title: New Student Registration Guide
content: # New Student Registration Guide

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
## Spring 2026

This guide walks you through the basics of registering for classes for the first time. We’ve loaded it with how-tos, additional videos and resources that we encourage you to explore.

If you have any questions or need additional assistance, contact the [Bronco Advising Center](../bronco-advising-center/index.shtml).

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### Registration Windows

* For every term you register, you have what’s called a registration window. It’s a specific day and time that you are able to begin to register for classes in an upcoming term. It is also sometimes called your enrollment appointment, registration appointment, enrollment date or enrollment time.
* In your BroncoDirect Student Center, find the Registration Window box on the right-hand side. This box will have your date and time. The date/time may not be visible until closer to your Orientation Day.
* For future terms, registration windows are posted about a week in advance of registration. Your registration window will be assigned based on the number of units you have successfully completed. You'll be able to enroll in courses in the timeframe of your registration window.

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### Unit Limits and Counts

* For your first semester at CPP, you'll register for classes at your Orientation Day. You will meet with staff and faculty to receive academic advising and they will walk you through the registration process.
* For every term you register, there are several opportunities to enroll in classes. All students have the ability to register during general registration, and the ability to adjust schedules during Add/Drop Period. Eligible students may also register during priority registration.
* Each opportunity also has specific unit limits. This is the number of units you are allowed to register for. See below for the unit limits you have – we'll explain more on Add/Drop Period and waitlisting later.

This table outlines the unit limits for each registration period.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Priority Registration | Up to 16 units |
| General Registration / Orientation Class Registration | Up to 16 units |
| Add/Drop Period | Up to 18 units |
| Waitlist Units | 10 units (of overall unit limit) |

* The amount of units for each class depends on what type of class it is – lab classes can be worth 1 to 2 units, while lecture classes can be 2 to 4 units. We’ll show you in the next step how to figure out how many units a class is worth.
* For future terms, you can find the unit enrollment limit on the [homepage of the Registration Guide](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/registration-page.shtml).

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### Holds

* Sometimes CPP will put a hold on your account when we need you to take care of something. Some [holds](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#hold) will prevent you from registering, like an Advising Hold or an SF Financial Hold. Other holds, like the Classes Saved hold, just prevent your courses from being [dropped](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) for non-payment.
* Your Orientation Services and Orientation Advising holds will be cleared as long as you've completed your Pre-Orientation modules and check in for your in-person program.
* Math/English Category Placement holds do not affect your ability to register for classes.

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### To Do List

* To Do list items are not holds and **do not** impact your ability to register.
* The To Do list is made up of checklist items such as university requests for documentation, including tax returns for your Financial Aid application, high school or college transcripts, official SAT scores, etc.
* Once the university has received a checklist item, that item will no longer display on your To Do list. It may take some time to clear.

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
## Using Schedule Builder to Register

You can use Schedule Builder can help you come up with potential schedules. You can also use Schedule Builder to import your choices to your BroncoDirect Shopping Cart. You can use the BroncoDirect Class Search to find classes, add them to your shopping cart and enroll.

After you import your choices to your BroncoDirect Shopping Cart, you'll need to finish enrolling. This video also shows you how to drop or swap courses.

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
## Optional: How to Register for Your Classes Using BroncoDirect

You can also use the BroncoDirect Class Search to find classes, add them to your shopping cart and enroll.
section: Optional: How to Register for Your Classes Using BroncoDirect

title: New Student Registration Guide
content: ## Additional Registration Information

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### Enrollment Error Messages

You may encounter enrollment error messages while registering. Here are some of the most common errors students see. If you have any questions about these error messages, contact the [Bronco Advising Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/bronco-advising-center/index.shtml).

This table explains what to do if you're getting an error message while trying to register.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Enrollment Error Message** | **What to Do** |
| [Hold](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#hold) on student's record, add not processed | Click on your [Holds](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#hold) box in your [Student Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#bdsc) to see if there are any holds that might prevent your registration. The Hold details explain how to clear the hold or who to contact. |
| Invalid [enrollment appointment](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#en) and open enrollment period has not begun | Double check your enrollment window day/time in your Enrollment Dates box. |
| [Requisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#re) not met for class, not enrolled | Look at the [Registrar's Office requisite error chart](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/files/public/RequisiteErrorChart.pdf). |
| [Unit limit](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#uel) exceeded for appointment period | You're trying to add a course that exceeds the [unit limit](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#uel) for your [enrollment window](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#en). |
| Time scheduling conflict, student not enrolled in class | You're trying to [add a course](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#add) that conflicts with another course in your schedule. You can submit a [Schedule Conflict Override Petition](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/forms/forms.shtml), but you need approval from both course instructors. |
| Student not enrolled, class full | The class is full. You should enroll in a [different section](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#sec), or ask the class instructor for a [permission number](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#per). |
| Student not eligible to enroll for term | You're not eligible to enroll. If this is an error, contact the [Bronco Advising Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/bronco-advising-center/index.shtml). |

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### How the Waitlist Works

If a [section of a course](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#sec) you want to take is full, you can add yourself to the [waitlist](a-z.shtml#wait). Visit the [Registration Guide homepage](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/registration-page.shtml) and find the [unit enrollment limits](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#uel) for [waitlisting units](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#waitu) under Term Information. Waitlisted units count toward your overall enrollment limit. Here are a few things to be aware of about waitlisting:

* You cannot waitlist a class that has a [corequisite](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#co).
* You will not be moved off the waitlist for a class if you are already registered in another section of the same class.
* The waitlist is updated hourly through the [registration period](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#grp).
* When space is available, you will be enrolled in the course and notified by email.
* Check your class schedule in your [Student Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#bdsc) for your [waitlist position](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#waitp).

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### How Permission Numbers Work

If you're trying to enroll in a course that is full, requires consent, or requires [requisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#re) ([pre-](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#pre) or [corequisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#co)), you can ask for a class permission number. They are one-use codes and only usable in the term you get them. They are also sometimes called add codes. For assistance, contact the [Bronco Advising Center](../bronco-advising-center/index.shtml).

Depending on the type of permission number, you get it from the course instructor or the [academic department:](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#dept)

This table outlines which office or person to ask for a permission number.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Enrollment Request Message** | **Request a Permission Number from:** |
| Not enrolled, class is full | The course instructor. |
| [Requisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#re) not met for class, not enrolled | Confirm on your [Degree Progress Report](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#dpr) that you have met the [requisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#re). Then, visit the [academic department](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#dept) to request a permission number. |
| Permission to enroll in this class is required | The course instructor. |
| Instructor consent required to enroll in class, add not processed | The course instructor. |

title: New Student Registration Guide
content: ## After Orientation and Prepping for Your First Day
section: After Orientation and Prepping for Your First Day

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### Check Your Instruction Modes

BroncoDirect and Schedule Builder will tell you the modes of instruction for your courses. Check the format your courses are being offered in by referencing the [Instruction Modes guide](instruction-modes.shtml).

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### How Add/Drop Works

* During the [Add/Drop Period](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#addp), you can add courses to your schedule or drop courses without them being recorded on your transcript. Refer to the [Student Success Central Academic Calendar](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/academic-calendar/index.shtml) for specific Add/Drop dates.  Here's a timeline for dropping courses:

This table outlines the different drop periods and what they mean.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **T****imeline/Deadline** | **Type of Drop** | **How** **To** **Drop** |
| From the 1st day of registration through the 10th day of the semester | You can [drop classes](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) without getting Ws on your transcript. | Drop in your [BroncoDirect Student Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#bdsc). |
| From the 10th day of the semester (second week) through the 20th day of the semester (fourth week) | You can [drop classes](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) with a grade of W. Undergraduate students are limited to 28 quarter units of recorded course [withdrawals](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#with) or 18 semester units of recorded course withdrawals, i.e., where students receive "W" grades in the classes dropped. | Drop in your [BroncoDirect Student Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#bdsc). |
| From the 20th day of the semester (fifth week) through the 12th week of the semester | If you need to [drop classes](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) after the 20th day of the semester because of illness or other problems of a serious nature beyond your control, you may request that those course [withdrawals](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#with) not contribute to the 28 quarter-unit or 18 semester-unit limit of recorded course withdrawals.   Upon review of documentation, substantiating the reason for class withdrawal is of serious nature beyond your control, your College Dean may approve that such course withdrawals not contribute to the limit. | Fill out [Request for Class Withdrawal](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/forms/forms.shtml) petition and attach supporting documentation.    You should continue to attend classes and submit coursework until you receive a decision. |
| After the 12th week of the semester through the last day of finals week | You can [drop](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) all of your courses only for serious and compelling reasons clearly beyond your control.    You should request to drop your entire schedule. Failure in a class or changes to your schedule are not acceptable reasons for withdrawing.   You may request that such course withdrawals not contribute to the limit of recorded course withdrawals. | Fill out [Request for Class Withdrawal](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/forms/forms.shtml) petition and attach supporting documentation.   **You should continue to attend classes and submit coursework until you receive a decision.** |

Find more information about withdrawals on the [Office of Student Success website](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/oss/academic-advising/university-policy-information/withdrawals.shtml).

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### Refund Policy

You are only entitled to a full tuition and fees refund if you cancel your registration or drop all of your courses before the first day of classes. After the first day of class you are entitled to a pro-rated refund determined by the date of your withdrawal. You may be entitled to a lower tuition fee if you dropped enough units to become a part-time student. To calculate this amount, visit the [Student Accounting & Cashiering Services](https://www.cpp.edu/student-accounting/tuition-fees/refund_policy.shtml) website select the term that you need a refund for.

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### How to Find Your Textbooks

Learn more about [Instant Access Complete program](https://www.broncobookstore.com/instant-access-complete.asp) on the Bronco Bookstore website.

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### The Importance of Reading Syllabi

A syllabus is a document that outlines the course policies and expectations. It includes your professor's contact information as well as their office hours. Grading policies, assignment due dates, the course schedule, required materials and other items may also be included. You'll receive syllabi ahead of or on the first day of class.

title: New Student Registration Guide
content:
### What to Expect at the Start of the Term

* Keep tabs on your Canvas homepage. Your instructor has to turn the courses on, so hang tight if you don't see them yet. Canvas updates itself nightly with any changes in your enrollment.
* Many instructors drop students who do not attend their class on the first day of instruction to allow for waitlisted students to add the course. However, do not rely on professors to drop you from classes you no longer wish to take. If you don't attend a class you are enrolled in, you may receive a grade of WU or F.
  + If a religious holiday conflicts with the first day of class, find more information on the [Dean of Students website](https://www.cpp.edu/deanofstudents/events/index.shtml).
* Office hours are the time that professors set aside to help students understand the course material and assignments. Attending office hours will allow you to get a better understanding of the course material and assignments as well as build a relationship with your faculty. Check your syllabi for your professors' office hours.
section: What to Expect at the Start of the Term

title: Registration: Meeting With Your Advisor
content: # Registration: Meeting With Your Advisor

title: Registration: Meeting With Your Advisor
content: ## Step 2: Meeting with Your Advisor

title: Registration: Meeting With Your Advisor
content:
### Know who to ask for help.

There are a few different types of advisors at Cal Poly Pomona.
section: Know who to ask for help.

title: Registration: Meeting With Your Advisor
content:
### Professional Staff Advisors

Professional staff advisors are full-time college-based staff.  This means that they are typically located in a central area of your college and have broad knowledge of the majors, but they are experts in campus policies, procedures, resources, and class selection. You can find contact information for the professional staff advisors in [Student Success Central’s Advising directory](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/directories/advising-directory.shtml).

title: Registration: Meeting With Your Advisor
content:
### Faculty Advisors

Faculty advisors are experts on their[curriculum](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#curr). They can help you with research and internship opportunities, as well as career options and graduate school. You can find faculty contact information on your [department's website](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/directories/departments-az.shtml).

title: Registration: Meeting With Your Advisor
content:
### Support Advisors

International students can also find information and resources at the [International Center](http://www.cpp.edu/~international/). [EOP students](https://www.cpp.edu/ssep/eop/index.shtml) have access to an EOP academic advisor. If you're a Bronco Athletics student-athlete, you have [an advisor](http://www.broncoathletics.com/staff.aspx?staff=8) that manages your academic support program and monitors your academic progress in relation to NCAA, CCAA and university requirements.

title: Registration: Meeting With Your Advisor
content:
### Bronco Advising Center

Bronco Advising Center (BAC) advisors can answer your questions about your class registration, academic record, financial aid package and account balance. You can find their contact information on the [BAC website](../../bronco-advising-center/index.shtml).

title: Registration: Meeting With Your Advisor
content:
### Be prepared for an advising session.

**Schedule an advising appointment** -- use [CPP Connect](https://www.cpp.edu/cppconnect) to get in touch with your assigned advisor.  Contact your [academic department](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#dept) to find your faculty advisor's [office hours.](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#oh)

**Know what to bring with you** -- don't show up to an advising appointment unprepared. If you’re attending an advising session in order to decide what to take next[term](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#term), you should already have an idea of what courses you might register for. You should also bring a copy of your flowchart and [curriculum sheet](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#currs), and know what requirements you've already completed. If you have a lot of questions, you might want to write them down so you don’t forget them. Finally, you always need to know your Bronco ID number in case your advisor needs to look up your academic record.
section: Be prepared for an advising session.

title: Registration: Meeting With Your Advisor
content:
you always need to know your Bronco ID number in case your advisor needs to look up your academic record.

title: Registration: Planning Your Courses
content: # Registration: Planning Your Courses

title: Registration: Planning Your Courses
content: ## Step 1: Planning Your Courses

title: Registration: Planning Your Courses
content:
### First things first — assemble your academic planning tools.

Use these tools to figure out your course options for the upcoming term.

**Get out your copy of [your curriculum sheet](https://www.cpp.edu/~academic-programs/academic-advising/tools/sheets-roadmaps/index.shtml)**— it tells you what courses you need to take to satisfy your degree.

If you have one, **pull out your [roadmap or flowchart](https://www.cpp.edu/~academic-programs/academic-advising/tools/sheets-roadmaps/index.shtml)**— it tells you when you should be taking courses.

**Bring up the [General Education (GE)](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#ge)course listing for any GE Areas you need to fulfill** — the [University Catalog](https://catalog.cpp.edu/) gives you all of your options. Not all of the courses in a particular GE area will be offered every term.

**Check your [Degree Progress Report](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#dpr)** — it's a tool in your [BroncoDirect Student Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#bdsc) that gives you a visual of your completed degree requirements. Watch our how-to video below to learn how to use it.

**Consult the [University Catalog](https://catalog.cpp.edu/)**— double check that you've satisfied [prerequisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#pre) or planned the [corequisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#co) of the courses you're thinking of taking.
section: First things first — assemble your academic planning tools.

title: Registration: Planning Your Courses
content:
### Plan for 30 semester units in one academic year.

Get in gear and Take 30 semester [units](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#unit) a year! Most [majors](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#major) require 180 quarter units or 120 semester units to complete.

Enroll in 30 semester units every year to graduate in four years (for freshmen) or two years (for transfer students).

It's up to you if you want to take 15 units each semester, or save some units to take in the summer.

Visit the [Take 30 website](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/take30/index.shtml) to learn more about the benefits of planning for 30 semester units.
section: Plan for 30 semester units in one academic year.

title: Registration: Planning Your Courses
content:
### Then, check for registration holds in BroncoDirect.

Some holds will prevent you from registering, like an SF Financial Hold or Title IX Hold. Other holds, like the Classes Saved hold, just prevent your courses from being dropped. Watch our how-to video below to learn how to check your holds.
section: Then, check for registration holds in BroncoDirect.

content:

title: Registration: Registering for Courses
content: # Registration: Registering for Courses

title: Registration: Registering for Courses
content: ## Step 3: Registering for Courses

title: Registration: Registering for Courses
content:
### Know your unit limits.

Visit the [Registration Guide homepage](http://www.cpp.edu/~studentsuccess/guides/registration-page.shtml) and find the Unit Enrollment Limits for a particular term under Term Information.

title: Registration: Registering for Courses
content:
### Find your registration window.

In your [BroncoDirect Student Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#bdsc), find the Registration Window box on the right-hand side. This box will have your [start date and time](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#en). They are posted about a week in advance of [registration](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#grp). You'll be able to enroll in courses in the timeframe of your [r](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#en)egistration window.

title: Registration: Registering for Courses
content:
### Search for classes.

To search for classes, click on the Search link in the upper left-hand side of your Student Center. In the Class Search, you must select the term and a subject. This will give you the full course offering listing for this subject.

To find specific classes, you can put in additional criteria. Many students put in a [course number](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#conu), or search for specific [General Education](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#ge) courses. Watch the how-to video below to learn about the additional criteria.

If you're looking for [waitlisted sections](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#waitc) of a specific course, make sure the "Show [Open Courses](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#open) Only" box is unchecked.

title: Registration: Registering for Courses
content:
### Add courses to your shopping cart and register.

To access your enrollment shopping cart, click on the Enroll link in your Student Center.

You're able to add courses to your shopping cart before your registration window, but you won't be able to enroll in them before your window begins. Before your window begins, make sure that the courses you've put in your shopping cart are still open.

To add courses to your shopping cart, enter the course number in the Shopping Cart menu or use the Class Search's Enroll button.

When your window begins, click on Proceed to Step 2 of 3. Confirm that you want to enroll in the courses you've selected, and click on Finish Enrolling. If you need to take co-requisite classes, like PHY 1520 and PHY 1520L, register for them at the same time and make sure the sections you've selected both have open seats.

The View Results page will confirm that you've successfully enrolled in the course, or give you an error message explaining why you can't enroll. View the GIF below to see the entire process.
section: Add courses to your shopping cart and register.

title: Registration: Registering for Courses
content:
### Know how to drop or waitlist a course.

During your registration window, you can drop courses from your schedule if you decide not to take them. To drop a course you're already enrolled in, click on the Drop tab in your Shopping Cart. Select the course(s) you want to drop, and then click on Drop Selected Classes. Confirm your choices, and click on Finish Dropping.

If a section of a course you want to take is full, you can add yourself to the waitlist. Visit the Registration Guide homepage and find the Unit Enrollment Limits for waitlisting units under Term Information. Waitlisted units count toward your overall enrollment limit.

To waitlist for a course, use the Class Search and check the "Waitlist if class is full" box. Here are a few things to be aware of about waitlisting:

* You cannot waitlist for a class with a [corequisite.](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#co)
  You will not be moved off the waitlist for a class if you are already registered in another section.
  The waitlist is updated hourly through the [registration period](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#grp).
  Be sure to drop any [waitlisted classes](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#waitc) once you've finalized your schedule to make sure you're not accidentally registered in a course.
  Check your class schedule in your [Student Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#bdsc) for your [number on the waitlist](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#waitp).
section: Know how to drop or waitlist a course.

title: Registration: Registering for Courses
content:
### Know how to troubleshoot enrollment errors.

You may encounter enrollment [error messages](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#err) while registering. Here are some of the most common errors students see. If you have any questions about these error messages, contact the [Bronco Advising Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/bronco-advising-center/index.shtml).

Enrollment Error Messages

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Enrollment Error Message** | **What to Do** |
| [Hold](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#hold) on student's record, add not processed | Click on your [Holds](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#hold) box in your [Student Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#bdsc) to see if there are any holds that might prevent your registration. The Hold details explain how to clear the hold or who to contact. |
| Invalid registration window and open enrollment period has not begun | Double check your [r](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#en)egistration window day/time in your Enrollment Dates box. |
| [Requisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#re) not met for class, not enrolled | Look at the [Registrar's Office requisite error chart](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/files/public/RequisiteErrorChart.pdf). |
| [Unit limit](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#uel) exceeded for appointment period | You're trying to add a course that exceeds the [unit limit](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#uel) for your [r](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#en)egistration window. |
| Time scheduling conflict, student not enrolled in class | You're trying to [add a course](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#add) that conflicts with another course in your schedule. You can submit a [Schedule Conflict Override Petition](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/forms/forms.shtml), but you need approval from both course instructors. |
| Student not enrolled, [class full](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#closed) | The [class is full](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#closed). You should enroll in a [different section](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#sec), or ask the class instructor for a [permission number](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#per). |
| Student not eligible to enroll for term | You're not eligible to enroll. If this is an error, contact the [Bronco Advising Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/bronco-advising-center/index.shtml). |
section: Know how to troubleshoot enrollment errors.

title: Registration: Registering for Courses
content:
### Learn how to use a permission number.

If you're trying to enroll in a course that is full, requires consent, or requires [requisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#re) ([pre-](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#pre) or [corequisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#co)), you can ask for a class [permission number](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#per). They are one-use codes and only usable in the [term](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#term) you get them. Depending on the type of permission number, you get it from the course instructor or the [academic department](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#dept). For assistance, contact the [Bronco Advising Center](http://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/bac/).

Troubleshooting Enrollment Errors

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Enrollment Request Message** | **Request a Permission Number from:** |
| Not enrolled, class is full | The course instructor. |
| [Requisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#re) not met for class, not enrolled | Confirm on your [Degree Progress Report](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#dpr) that you have met the [requisites](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#re). Then, visit the [academic department](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#dept) to request a permission number. |
| Permission to enroll in this class is required | The course instructor. |
| Instructor consent required to enroll in class, add not processed | The course instructor. |

Once you receive a permission number, use the Class Search to find the course. Make sure to uncheck "Show Open Classes Only". Enter the number in the Permission Nbr box. If you have a permission number to override the waitlist, **do not** select the Wait list if class is full box -- this will deactive the number. If the permission number is successful, the course will be added to your Shopping Cart.
section: Learn how to use a permission number.

title: Registration: Registering for Courses
content:
### Check your schedule.

Before your registration window is over, make sure that you're enrolled in the courses you want to be enrolled in. It's your responsibility to drop any courses you don't want to take. If you're on the waitlist for a course, keep an eye on your Course Schedule to see if you move up the waitlist.

title: Registration: Starting the Term
content: # Registration: Starting the Term

title: Registration: Starting the Term
content: ## Step 4: Starting the Term

title: Registration: Starting the Term
content:
### Make adjustments during Add/Drop Period.

During [Add/Drop Period](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#addp), you can [add courses](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#add) to your schedule or [drop courses](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) without them being recorded on your transcript. Refer to the [Student Success Central Academic Calendar](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/calendar/) for specific Add/Drop dates.  Here's a timeline for dropping courses:

Dropping Courses

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Timeline/Deadline** | **Type of Drop** | **How To Drop** |
| From the 1st day of registration through the 10th day of the semester | You can [drop classes](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) without getting Ws on your transcript. | Drop in your [BroncoDirect Student Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#bdsc). |
| From the 10th day of the semester (second week) through the 20th day of the semester (fourth week) | You can [drop classes](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) with a grade of W. Undergraduate students are limited to 28 quarter units of recorded course [withdrawals](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#with) or 18 semester units of recorded course [withdrawals](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#with), i.e., where students receive "W" grades in the classes dropped. | Drop in your [BroncoDirect Student Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#bdsc). |
| From the 20th day of the semester (fifth week) through the 12th week of the semester | If you need to [drop classes](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) after the 20th day of the semester because of illness or other problems of a serious nature beyond your control, you may request that those course [withdrawals](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#with) not contribute to the 28 quarter-unit or 18 semester-unit limit of recorded course withdrawals.  Upon review of documentation, substantiating the reason for class withdrawal is of serious nature beyond your control, your College Dean may approve that such course withdrawals not contribute to the limit. | 1. Fill out [Request for Class Withdrawal](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/forms/forms.shtml) petition and attach supporting documentation.  **You should continue to attend classes and submit coursework until you receive a decision.** |
| After the 12th week of the semester through the last day of finals week | You can [drop](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) all of your courses only for serious and compelling reasons clearly beyond your control.  You should request to drop your entire schedule. Failure in a class or changes to your schedule are not acceptable reasons for withdrawing.  You may request that such course withdrawals not contribute to the limit of recorded course withdrawals. | 1. Fill out [Request for Class Withdrawal](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/forms/forms.shtml) petition and attach supporting documentation.  **You should continue to attend classes and submit coursework until you receive a decision.** |

**Types of Withdrawals**

**W (Withdrawal)**—this grade indicated that you were permitted to [drop that course](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#drop) after the [Add/Drop period](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#addp). It carries no meaning as far as your performance in the class. Receiving a [W](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#withdrawal) does not count as an “attempt” at taking a course and is not considered a course repetition.

**WX**—this grade indicates that you withdrew from a course with a serious and compelling reason and that the withdrawal won’t contribute to the 28 quarter-unit or 18 semester-unit limit on withdrawals. This grade is converted to a [W](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#withdrawal) on your official transcript.

**WU (Withdrawal Unauthorized)**—the grade of a WU is given if you discontinue attendance and participation in a course without officially dropping the course and is equivalent to the grade of an F. It is your responsibility to drop courses that you do not plan to attend.

**Retroactive Withdrawal**—If you receive all WU or F grades in all of your coursework for a term [you may petition to](https://catalog.cpp.edu/content.php?catoid=51&navoid=3876#Retroactive_Withdrawal) the Retroactive Withdrawal Committee to get those grades changes to [Ws](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#withdrawal) if you can show documentation of the serious and compelling reasons that those grades were received. You can apply for this up to one year from the last day of the term in question.

title: Registration: Starting the Term
content:
### Know the refund policy.

You are only entitled to a full tuition and fees refund if you cancel your registration or drop all of your courses before the first day of classes. After the first day of class you are entitled to a pro-rated refund determined by the date of your withdrawal. You may be entitled to a lower tuition fee if you dropped enough units to become a part-time student. To calculate your refund, visit the [Student Accounting & Cashiering Services website](https://www.cpp.edu/student-accounting/tuition-fees/refund_policy.shtml) and select the term that you need a refund for.

title: Registration: Starting the Term
content:
### Attend class on the first day.

Many instructors drop students who do not attend their class on the first day of instruction to allow for waitlisted students to add the course. Do not rely on professors to drop you from classes you no longer wish to take. If you don't attend a class you are enrolled in, you'll still receive a grade of WU/F. If a religious holiday conflicts with the first day of class, Cal Poly Pomona will accommodate you as outlined in the Dean of Students’ [Reasonable Accommodations webpage](https://www.cpp.edu/deanofstudents/events.shtml).
section: Attend class on the first day.

title: Registration: Starting the Term
content:
### Keep in touch with your advisor.

Make sure you continue to update your academic plan with your advisor to make sure you remain on track for [graduation](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#grad).
section: Keep in touch with your advisor.

title: Registration: Starting the Term
content:
### Attend your professors' office hours.

Attending [office hours](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#oh) will allow you to get a better understanding of the course material and assignments as well as build a relationship with your faculty. Check your [syllabi](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#syl) for office hours, or contact the [academic department](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/a-z.shtml#dept).

title: Registration: Starting the Term
content:
### Know your resources.

You have many places on campus that you can reach out to when you need help:

* The [Student Success Central Website](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/index.shtml)— On this website, you can find information on different academic programs, advising & academic resources, how-to videos, and more. Use the navigation at the top.
* The [Bronco Advising Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/bronco-advising-center/index.shtml)— This is the place you can go to have help without traveling to several different offices. The advisors can solve registration problems, check on your petitions, teach you how to use your online academic tools, and anything else you might need help with.
* [PolyTransfer](https://www.cpp.edu/polytransfer/)— Transfer students can get peer mentoring, join a study hall with other transfer students and receive 1-on-1 student coaching and support.
* The [Learning Resource Center](https://www.cpp.edu/lrc/)— Located on the second floor of the University Library, the LRC offers free tutoring for many different classes both online and in-person, workshops for math, science, and business classes, and test preparation resources.
* The [International Center](http://www.cpp.edu/international/)— International students can come here to find resources and forms that affect their immigration standing, visa benefits, health insurance, and taxes.
* The [Disability Resource Center](http://www.cpp.edu/drc/)— The DRC provides accommodations and support services to students with disabilities, such as testing, alternate format, notetaking, interpreting/captioning, and mobility assistance, to ensure that you have an equal opportunity to succeed.
* [Educational Opportunity Program](https://www.cpp.edu/ssep/eop/index.shtml)— EOP offers academic advising, peer advising, tutorial services, grants, and a study lounge to low income, first generation students from historically disadvantaged backgrounds.

title: Registration: Starting the Term
content:
advising, peer advising, tutorial services, grants, and a study lounge to low income, first generation students from historically disadvantaged backgrounds.

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content: ## Applying for Graduation FAQs

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### The Differences Between Graduation and Commencement

Graduation is the term in which you have successfully completed all of your degree requirements.

Commencement is the college ceremonies in May. Any student who receives a Cal Poly Pomona degree during the academic year (fall, spring and summer) is automatically eligible to participate in that year’s Commencement ceremony. Visit the [Commencement website](https://www.cpp.edu/commencement/index.shtml) for up-to-date information.
section: The Differences Between Graduation and Commencement

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### How do I know if I'm ready to apply for graduation?

* You're an active student.
* You're going to complete all of your remaining degree requirements in the next term, based on your curriculum sheet, your unofficial transcript and your Degree Progress Report. If something doesn't look right on your DPR, use [this tool](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/guides/dpr.shtml) to troubleshoot.
* You've worked with your academic advisor to ensure you're going to complete all of your remaining degree requirements. You can find the advising directory [here](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/directories/advising-directory.shtml).
* You have a minimum 2.0 overall, major, and Cal Poly Pomona GPA.
* You've taken at least 30 semester units or 50 quarter units in residence (at Cal Poly Pomona).
* You will complete any internship or senior project requirements for your major by the end of the term.
section: How do I know if I'm ready to apply for graduation?

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### When I'm ready, how do I apply?

You can apply for graduation in your BroncoDirect Student Center. Watch the how-to video below for instructions.

You can submit an application if you either have senior standing now, will have senior standing at the end of the current term, or have graduate standing. If you are not eligible for graduation, the Apply for Graduation link will not be visible. If you think you are eligible to apply, but you don’t see the link, contact the Registrar’s Office at registrar@cpp.edu.

After you apply for graduation, you'll receive a confirmation at your CPP email address.

There are specific timeframes for graduation in each term. See below for specific dates for upcoming terms, and the [Registrar's Office website](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/graduation/applying-for-graduation.shtml) for more graduation-related dates.

Graduation Timelines

| **If you want to graduate in:** | **Access application in BroncoDirect beginning:** | **To get the graduating senior registration window, submit application by:** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Summer 2025** | December 1, 2024 | March 7, 2025 |
| **Fall 2025** | January 1, 2025 | March 28, 2025 |
| **Spring 2026** | August 1, 2025 | October 6, 2025 |
| **Summer 2026** | December 1, 2025 | February 27, 2026 |
| **Fall 2026** | January 1, 2026 | March 27, 2026 |

If you miss the online graduation application period, email the [Bronco Advising Center](../bronco-advising-center/index.shtml). Do not apply online if you do not see the semester you need to apply for.
section: When I'm ready, how do I apply?

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### Once I apply, what are the next steps?

* The Registrar's Office will review your application. You'll get a communication when this happens. You will also see an update at the top of your Degree Progress Report.
* After you get this communication, you can pay your graduation fee. It will be added as a charge to your account balance.
* Keep tabs on your CPP email. The Registrar's Office will communicate any updates to you.
* Review your personal information in BroncoDirect. Make sure that everything is up to date. Your diploma will have your Primary Name, which is on your official documents. Find more info about updating your name on your diploma on the [Registrar's Office website](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/graduation/diploma-certificate-information.shtml).
* Keep in touch with your [academic advisor](../directories/advising-directory.shtml). You can work with your academic advisor to ensure that you're on track to graduate.
* Turn in any outstanding paperwork. You must turn in any transcripts, petitions, and other paperwork ASAP.
* Work hard to pass your last courses. The [Learning Resource Center](https://www.cpp.edu/lrc/) has subject and writing tutoring for many different courses. Think about starting a study group, and make sure you attend instructor office hours if you have any questions about a course.
section: Once I apply, what are the next steps?

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content: ## Graduation Application Review Process

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### You Apply or Are Auto Applied

You'll receive an email when your application has been received.
A Graduation Application Hold will be applied to your Student Center. This hold does not impact your ability to register for your final term. It will only prevent registration in the terms after you are expected to graduate.
section: You Apply or Are Auto Applied

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### Initial Review

If the Registrar's Office finds you are on track to meet your requirements, you will receive an email indicating your application has been Approved. Your student account will be charged with the graduation fee. Your Degree Progress Report will show an Approved status. If the Registrar's Office finds that you have outstanding requirements, you will receive an email indicating your application is In Review. If you haven’t already done so, please meet with your academic advisor to review and resolve these remaining requirements. If you need to withdraw your application for more time to complete degree requirements, you can work with your academic advisor to request to move your application to a future term.

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### After Your Final Term

After your final term's grades are posted, the Registrar's Office does a final review. If the Registrar's Office finds you meet all of your degree requirements, you will receive an email indicating your degree has been Awarded. You will receive an email when your degree has been awarded. Your Degree Progress Report and unofficial transcript will show this, and you'll get a second email about your diploma. If the Registrar's Office finds that you have outstanding requirements, you will receive an email indicating your application is Pending.  You should address the outstanding requirements as soon as possible, but during the graduation clearance period for the term.

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content: ## Application Review FAQs

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### How can I check the status of my graduation application?

* You complete your final requirements in your graduation term. This includes passing your classes, completing any internship or senior project requirements, finishing your residency units, and keeping your cumulative and CPP GPAs at or above a 2.0. You should also make sure you send in any transcripts if you're taking final classes at other institutions or submit any outstanding AP or IB scores.
* You will also receive a registration hold for the following term. Review the hold details if you have additional questions.
* After final grades post for the term, the Registrar's Office will begin awarding degrees. The Registrar's Office begins the degree awarding process the week following the final grade deadline. It takes time for each Transfer and Graduation Advisor to review each student's academic record. If something is missing or cannot account for all of your requirements, the Registrar's Office will send you an email.
* Meanwhile, you should submit any outstanding paperwork. This includes petitions, transcripts, or other paperwork your advisor has recommended that you submit.
* When your degree is awarded, the Registrar's Office will send you two emails: a congratulatory email and a senior exit survey. At this point, your official transcript will reflect that you have graduated.
* You'll receive a copy of your diploma in the mail within 3-4 weeks. You should confirm that all of your personal information is up-to-date in your Student Center.
section: How can I check the status of my graduation application?

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### How can I request proof of my degree before my degree is officially awarded?

If you need an official letter of expected graduation, email the Bronco Advising Center at bac@cpp.edu.

If you need proof of degree for a graduate school program or potential employer before your degree is officially awarded, email the Bronco Advising Center at bac@cpp.edu with the following information:

* Your name
* Your Bronco ID number
* Proof from the graduate school program or employer requesting proof of degree
* Any deadlines the graduate school program or employer have given you

Please allow up to 3-5 business days for us to receive and review your request. If you are missing any of the above information, it may take up to an additional 10-14 business days for us to award your degree.

The Registrar's Office begins the degree awarding process the week following the final grade deadline. Please note that we will not be able to process your request until the term has ended, your final grades are posted and the Registrar's Office has begun their degree awarding process.
section: How can I request proof of my degree before my degree is officially awarded?

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### How can I make sure my personal information (i.e. my name on my diploma,  my mailing address, etc.) is correct?

You can edit much of your personal information in your BroncoDirect Student Center. Visit the [Registrar's Office website](https://www.cpp.edu/registrar/student-record/update-personal-information.shtml) for more information and instructions.
section: How can I make sure my personal information (i.e. my name on my diploma,  my mailing address, etc.) is correct?

title: Steps to Senior Success: Everything You Need to Know About Graduation at CPP
content:
### If I have questions about graduation, who do I reach out to?

If you're graduating and have questions about your application, email tga@cpp.edu with your name, Bronco ID number and a brief explanation.
section: If I have questions about graduation, who do I reach out to?

title: Guided Registration
content: # Guided Registration

title: Guided Registration
content:
## Understanding Your English and Math Categories

If you are joining CPP as a first-time freshman, you are required to complete your GE Area 1A (English Composition) and GE Area 2 (Mathematical Concepts) within your first year.

If you have questions or need assistance making course adjustments, contact the [Bronco Advising Center](../bronco-advising-center/index.shtml).
section: Understanding Your English and Math Categories

title: Guided Registration
content:
## Finding Your Categories

1. Log into your Bronco Applicant Portal. and go to Application To Do List.
2. Click on the link for the Category you want to know more about. The details will tell you what Category you are placed in.

**Note:** These category placement holds do not prevent you from registering for classes.

title: Guided Registration
content: ## English Categories

title: Guided Registration
content:
### English Category 1

**Note: This category placement hold does not prevent you from registering for classes.**

You have completed your General Education Area 1A (English Composition) through AP/IB or transfer coursework.

Review your curriculum sheet and consult with your academic advsior to see if there are additional English courses needed for your major requirements.

title: Guided Registration
content:
### English Category 2 or 2C

**Note: This category placement hold does not prevent you from registering for classes.**

You are ready to enroll in a General Education Area 1A (English Composition) course. Look in your CPP email for your personalized link to the Directed Self-Placement Survey (DSP), which will help you determine which English course you should enroll in. Complete the DSP by your Orientation date.

With your DSP results, you may be guided to register for a course in Fall 2025, or you will be required to register for it in Spring 2026.

If you are interested in the Interdisciplinary General Education track, talk to an advisor during your Orientation program.

If your DSP score recommends placement into a multilingual (M) section of a course, you may be directed to courses with the M designation.

title: Guided Registration
content:
### English Category 2 to 3

**Note: This category placement hold does not prevent you from registering for classes.**

Based on your Directed Self Placement score, you are recommended to enroll in a stretch General Education Area 1A (English Composition) course. You will be guided to register for a course in Fall 2025.

You will be required to take a second lecture course in Spring 2026 to complete your 1A requirement.

If your DSP score recommends placement into a multilingual (M) section of a course, you may be directed to courses with the M designation.

title: Guided Registration
content:
### English Category 3 or 4

**Note: This category placement hold does not prevent you from registering for classes.**

You are ready to enroll in a stretch General Education Area 1A (English Composition) course. Look in your CPP email for your personalized link to the Directed Self-Placement Survey (DSP), which will help you determine which English course you should enroll in. Complete the DSP by your Orientation date.

With your DSP results, you will be guided to register for a course in Fall 2025. You will be required to take a second lecture course in Spring 2026 to complete your 1A requirement.

If your DSP score recommends placement into a multilingual (M) section of a course, you may be directed to courses with the M designation.

title: Guided Registration
content:
### Math Category 1

**Note: This category placement hold does not prevent you from registering for classes.**

You have completed your General Education Area 2 (Mathematical Concepts) through AP/IB or transfer coursework.

Review your curriculum sheet and consult with your academic advsior to see if there are additional math courses needed for your major requirements.

title: Guided Registration
content:
### Math Category 2 or 2C

**Note: This category placement hold does not prevent you from registering for classes.**

You are ready to enroll in a General Education Area 2 (Mathematical Concepts) course.

You may be guided to register for a course in Fall 2025, or you will be required to register for it in Spring 2026. During your Orientation advising session, consult an academic advisor if you have questions about the GE math class you should take for your major requirements.

If you received communication about the ALEKS PPL calculus placement assessment, take it ASAP ahead of your Orientation Day.

title: Guided Registration
content:
### Math Category 3

**Note: This category placement hold does not prevent you from registering for classes.**

You are ready to enroll in a supported General Education Area 2 (Mathematical Concepts) course. You will be guided to register in a lecture and activity course for Fall 2025. You may be required to take a second lecture course in Spring 2026 to complete your Area 2 requirement.

title: Guided Registration
content:
### Math Category 4

**Note: This category placement hold does not prevent you from registering for classes.**

You are ready to enroll in a supported General Education Area 2 (Mathematical Concepts) course. You will be guided to register in a lecture and activity course for Fall 2025. You may be required to take a second lecture course in Spring 2026 to complete your Area 2 requirement.

If you are participating in the [Bronco Scholars 6-week math course](https://www.cpp.edu/bsp): You will begin a 2-part course series in Summer 2025, and will register for a course for Fall 2025 to complete your Area 2 requirement.

title: About Us
content:
## One Team, One Goal, Student Success

Using equity-minded, student-centered, data-informed strategies, the Office of Student Success at Cal Poly Pomona drives institutional transformation to facilitate persistence, retention, and graduation. We proactively connect students with advising, targeted academic support, and critical resources throughout their educational journey.

We lead a wide range of student-centered initiatives, including  Advising Technology, the Bronco Advising Center, Bronco Navigators, Bronco Scholars, College Access & Transitions, Early Alerts, the First-Year Experience, Graduation Pledge Programs, I Am First, the Learning Resource Center, PolyTransfer, RAMP, Student Success Communication, Undeclared Advising, and University Advising. These programs and services are strategically designed to empower students in achieving their goals.

Through our collaborative, intentional and strategic work, we develop a campus-wide network that offers timely, personalized guidance and fosters a sense of belonging. Aligned with Cal Poly Pomona’s mission and the CSU system’s strategic priorities, our efforts ensure that every student has equitable access to the opportunities they need to grow, excel, and graduate ready to thrive beyond the classroom.
section: One Team, One Goal, Student Success

title: About Us
content:
## What is student success at Cal Poly Pomona?

The mission of Cal Poly Pomona is to cultivate success through a diverse culture of experiential learning, discovery, and innovation.  Our vision is to be the model inclusive polytechnic university inspiring creativity and innovation, embracing local and global challenges, and transforming lives.  Cal Poly Pomona defines student success through an integrated polytechnic model that embraces a holistic view of student learning, engagement and growth; a paradigm that recognizes the student experience and opportunities for learning occur both in and out of the classroom.

Students’ cumulative experiences at Cal Poly Pomona are intentionally designed to engage with us in a life cycle that begins before matriculation and enrollment, and continues throughout degree pursuit and beyond graduation to career.  We recognize success for a Cal Poly Pomona student requires significant commitment from the student and the University.  The University provides world-class polytechnic programs where students learn to connect theory, practice, and research and apply it to real world problems.  Education is delivered through active, learning-centered approaches and experiential pedagogies.

Cal Poly Pomona is a highly diverse institution committed to the success of all of our students.  As such, the institution has set the eradication of the opportunity gap that currently exists between underrepresented students and those from non-underrepresented student populations as our top priority. The University views education through an equity lens and continuously seeks to reduce institutional barriers impeding students’ progress academically, socially, and developmentally.

At Cal Poly Pomona, we are committed to providing student experiences intended to develop skills to actively engage as a member of an inclusive community, effectively enlist personal competence, agency, and resilience, and to live a healthy and well life during and beyond college.  Cal Poly Pomona is focused on the holistic dimensions of student success and optimizing the likelihood our students ultimately realize their post-graduation goals.  A successful Cal Poly Pomona student possesses the polytechnic skills to face diverse challenges in the rapidly changing employment landscape, able to contribute solutions to the important issues facing our world, and prepared to positively engage with and contribute to the human enterprise.
section: What is student success at Cal Poly Pomona?

title: Academic Standing
content:
## What is Academic Standing?

Academic Standing is defined by your cumulative, Cal Poly Pomona and/or major grade point averages (GPAs). A GPA is a calculated average of the letter grades you earn. If you want to learn more about the letter grades CPP uses, visit the [University Catalog](https://catalog.cpp.edu/) (navigate to the Academic Policies section and click on Grading System).

You can check your GPAs on your unofficial transcript or Degree Progress Report. Both are located in your [BroncoDirect Student Center](https://my.cpp.edu/).

Your Academic Standing is updated each term after you receive your final grades.

Read the information below to learn about the different academic standings at Cal Poly Pomona that reflect your current progress and may impact your future enrollment.

title: Academic Standing
content: ## More About Academic Standing for Undergraduates
section: More About Academic Standing for Undergraduates

title: Academic Standing
content:
### Good Standing

#### What It Means

Your Cumulative and CPP GPA are 2.0 or above.

title: Academic Standing
content:
### Academic Warning (Formerly At-Risk)

#### What It Means

You have a CPP or Major GPA between a 2.0 and a 2.2.

#### What Will Happen

The Academic Warning hold is placed on your account, and the hold will impact your future registration.

#### Next Steps

In order to remove the hold, you must complete a Canvas based module. Once completed, the hold will be removed automatically within 48 hours.

title: Academic Standing
content:
### Academic Notice (Formerly Academic Probation)

#### What It Means

You have a Cumulative or CPP GPA below 2.0, but it is above the level for Academic Notice for Contract.

The first time you fall into Academic Notice for Contract standing (see requirements below), you will be placed on Academic Notice.

#### What Will Happen

The Academic Notice hold is placed on your account and the hold will impact your future registration.

#### Next Steps

In order to remove the hold, you must complete three steps:

1. Complete a Canvas-based module.
2. Complete the Academic Notice Worksheet linked at the end of the Canvas module.
3. Meet with your [Retention and Graduation Specialist](../../retention-graduation-specialists.shtml). After this meeting, the hold will be removed.

title: Academic Standing
content:
### Academic Notice with Contract (Formerly Academic Probation with Contract)

#### What It Means

You have a Cumulative or CPP GPA below the following levels based on class standing:

Table of Academic Notice with Contract GPAs by Class Standing

| Class Standing | Semester Units Completed | Grade Point Average |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Freshman | Less than 30 | <1.5 |
| Sophomore | 30-59 | <1.7 |
| Junior | 60-89 | <1.85 |
| Senior | 90 or more | <1.95 |

#### What Will Happen

The Academic Notice with Contract hold is placed on your account and the hold will impact your future registration. You should [use our Academic Notice with Contract tool](disqualification.shtml) to understand the specific impact for future terms.

#### Next Steps

In order to remove the hold, you must complete three steps:

1. Complete a Canvas-based module.
2. Complete an Academic Notice with Contract worksheet and contract linked at the end of the Canvas module.
3. Finally, meet with your [Retention and Graduation Specialist](../../retention-graduation-specialists.shtml). After this meeting, the hold will be removed.
section: Academic Notice with Contract (Formerly Academic Probation with Contract)

title: Academic Standing
content:
### Administrative Academic Notice (Formerly Administrative Probation)

You can be placed on Administrative Academic Notice if you are not making progress in your current major. You will receive a Student Success Milestone hold (SSM).  This hold does not prevent registration. The hold encourages you to schedule a meeting with your assigned advisor as soon as possible. In that meeting, you and your advisor will complete an Administrative Academic Notice agreement.

An example of Administrative Academic Notice may include if you have not been taking courses in your current major for at least one term, which may include courses for a major you have not declared.

If you do not meet the terms of the Administrative Academic Notice agreement, you may be discontinued from the university or have a hold placed that will prevent registration for future terms.

Please visit the University Catalog for details on Administrative Academic Notice.

title: Academic Standing
content:
### Disqualification

**What It Means**

Undergraduate students can fall into disqualification in two ways:

1. If you do not meet the term GPA of 2.0 or higher at the end of any term following your first Academic Notice with Contract status (for as long as you remain on Academic Notice with Contract status).
2. If, after your first Academic Notice with Contract status, you successfully raise your CPP and Overall GPA above the Academic Notice with Contract threshold, but then your GPA falls below the threshold in a subsequent term, you will be automatically disqualified from CPP.

**What Will Happen**

If you are academically disqualified, you will be discontinued from the university in the next term. This means that if you are enrolled in classes in the next term, you will be dropped from those classes. It also means that you will need to reapply to the institution if you want to enroll in the future as a matriculated student.

**Next Steps**

Students who are interested in continuing their education after academic disqualification have a few options.

If you are interested in returning, visit the [Returning Student website](https://www.cpp.edu/admissions/returning-students/index.shtml). Complete the form and a member of the Bronco Advising Center team will reach out to you to discuss options for readmissions. Options may include [repeating courses through Open University](https://www.cpp.edu/cpge/open-university/registration/index.shtml) or submitting a [Retroactive Withdrawal petition](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/oss/academic-advising/university-policy-information/retroactive-withdrawal.shtml), among others. Only after your CPP GPA has improved will you be eligible to reapply; however, improving your GPA above a 2.0 does not guarantee that you will be readmitted.

If returning to CPP is not an option, the Bronco Advising Center team member will also discuss options for applying to other institutions.

title: Academic Standing
content: ## More About Academic Standing for Graduates
section: More About Academic Standing for Graduates

title: Academic Standing
content:
### Good Standing

#### What It Means

Your Cumulative and CPP GPA are 3.0 or above.

title: Academic Standing
content:
### Academic Notice (Formerly Probation)

#### What It Means

You have a CPP or Major GPA below a 3.0, but it's above the level for Academic Notice with Contract.

#### What Will Happen

The Academic Notice hold is placed on your account and the hold will impact your future registration.

#### Next Steps

In order to remove the hold, you must complete two steps:

1. First, meet with your graduate advisor.
2. After meeting with your graduate advisor, you must have your hold removed by your graduate coordinator, department chair, or designee.

title: Academic Standing
content:
### Academic Notice with Contract (Formerly Probation with Contract)

#### What It Means

You have a Cumulative or CPP GPA below the following levels based on units completed:

Table of Academic Notice With Contract GPAs by Units Completed

| Semester Units Completed | Grade Point Average |
| --- | --- |
| 8 | <2.3 |
| 16 | <2.7 |
| 24 | <2.9 |

#### What Will Happen

The Academic Notice with Contract hold is placed on your account and the hold will impact your future registration.

#### Next Steps

In order to remove the hold, you must complete these steps:

1. Familiarize yourself with university and college academic policies and procedures. Additional information regarding academic standing may be obtained from the [University Catalog](https://catalog.cpp.edu/). For Catalog review, please scroll to the subheading in Academic Policies titled “Academic Standing – Postbaccalaureate Students.”
2. Complete the Academic Notice with Contract Worksheet and Academic Notice with Contract Agreement. Contact your graduate coordinator if you need assistance with filling out these forms.
3. Meet with your graduate coordinator or department chair immediately for academic advising to determine how to regain good standing. The Academic Notice with Contract worksheet and Agreement Form must be approved by your graduate coordinator.
   After meeting with your graduate coordinator, you must have the hold removed by your graduate coordinator, department chair, or designee.

title: Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness
content: # Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness

title: Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness
content: ## About Course Repetition and Grade Forgiveness
section: About Course Repetition and Grade Forgiveness

title: Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness
content:
### What is Course Repetition?

Course Repetition is when you take a course more than once. You can repeat a course with the goal of earning a better grade if you earned a non-passing grade in your previous attempt. All attempts appear on your transcript and are included in your GPA calculation.

title: Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness
content:
### What is Grade Forgiveness?

When you earn a better grade the second or third time, you may be eligible for Grade Forgiveness, which allows you to replace the previous grade with the new one. This means that the old grade will be taken out of your GPA, however all attempts will still appear on your transcript.

title: Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness
content: ## More About Course Repetition and Grade Forgiveness for Undergraduates
section: More About Course Repetition and Grade Forgiveness for Undergraduates

title: Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness
content:
### Which courses can I repeat?

You can repeat any course, but **only grades of C-, D+, D, D- F, NC, WU or IC may be repeated.**

A D- is considered passing. However, a grade of **C- or better** is required of each Cal Poly Pomona or transfer student completing courses in:

* Written communication in the English language (GE area A2)
* Oral communication in the English language (GE area A1)
* Critical thinking (GE area A3)
* Mathematics/quantitative reasoning (GE area B4)

You should refer to the [University Catalog](https://catalog.cpp.edu/) for any specific grade prerequisites for future courses.

title: Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness
content:
### How do I repeat a course?

You register for the course in a future term. Review the [Student Success Central academic calendar](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/calendar/index.shtml) for upcoming registration periods.
section: How do I repeat a course?

title: Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness
content:
### How many times can I repeat a course?

You can repeat a course no more than two times for a maximum of three attempts. The number of repeats of individual courses prior to Fall 2009 will contribute to this 2 repetition (3 attempts) limit.

You can submit a Petition to Waive Restrictions on Course Repeats if you want to repeat a class for a fourth attempt. Contact the [Bronco Advising Center](https://www.cpp.edu/studentsuccess/bronco-advising-center/index.shtml) to begin the process.

You are limited to **28 units** of course repetition.
section: How many times can I repeat a course?

title: Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness
content:
### How does grade forgiveness work?

Grade Forgiveness through course repetition will automatically happen a) when you repeat the course if the new grade is better than the original grade and b) if you have grade forgiveness units to use. You do not need to submit any paperwork.

The Registrar's Office will automatically process it after final grades are posted. You'll be able to check your GPA on your unofficial transcript in your BroncoDirect Student Center. Contact your academic advisor if you have have any questions or see a discrepancy.

title: Course Repetition & Grade Forgiveness
content:
### How often can I utilize grade forgiveness?

You are limited to **16 units** of grade forgiveness. This means that you will be able to forgive 16 semester units of courses for which you’ve earned a C- or lower.
section: How often can I utilize grade forgiveness?

Graduate students are not eligible for grade forgiveness.

title: Academic Notice with Contract (formerly Probation with Contract)
content: # Academic Notice with Contract (formerly Probation with Contract)
section: Academic Notice with Contract (formerly Probation with Contract)

title: Academic Notice with Contract (formerly Probation with Contract)
content:
## About

**Academic Notice with Contract (formerly Probation with Contract) occurs when the student's GPA reaches the levels specified below.** A student who is designated Academic Notice with Contract on the basis of their grade point average may not be allowed to attend Cal Poly Pomona for at least one term. If the student does not improve their academic standing, they may not be able to return to Cal Poly Pomona.

GPA Thresholds

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Quarter Units Completed | Semester Units Completed | Grade Point Average |
| Freshman | Less than 45 | Less than 30 | <1.5 |
| Sophomore | 45-89 | 30-59 | <1.7 |
| Junior | 90 - 134 | 60-89 | <1.85 |
| Senior | 135 or more | 90 or more | <1.95 |

Students are notified of this status as soon as possible after the end of the term.

Students have options available in order to regain their enrollment eligibility. **Students should visit their academic advisor IMMEDIATELY to discuss which option is best for them. International Students should also see their International Center Advisor for additional information.**

**To view your options for continuing your educational career at CPP, please select from the options below.**

title: Academic Notice with Contract (formerly Probation with Contract)
content:
## Academic Notice with Contract Processes

Which term were you placed on Academic Notice with Contract?

Select Option
Fall 2025
Spring or Summer 2025
Fall 2024
Spring or Summer 2024
Fall 2023
Spring 2023

title: Academic Notice with Contract (formerly Probation with Contract)
content:
## Fall 2025

Students who fall into Academic Notice with Contract at the end of Fall 2025 can enroll for Winter and Spring 2026. However, students also have the choice to sit-out during Spring 2026.

If the student enrolls in Spring 2026, the student will be required to complete several requirements to continue to enroll in future terms:

* The student must earn a 2.0 TERM GPA in Spring 2026 and all subsequent terms until they are no longer on Academic Notice with Contract.
* The student will be required to complete advising requirements prior to registering for Summer and Fall 2026 (and in all subsequent terms).
* These requirements include:
  + Completing an online Canvas module
  + Completing an advising worksheet
  + Meeting with their College Retention and Graduation Specialist

The student will not be able to register for more than 6 units in Summer 2026, and 13 units in Fall 2026, and in all subsequent terms until they earn a CPP and overall GPA of a 2.0.

If the student chooses to sit-out during Spring 2026, they will be required to complete the requirements listed above beginning in Fall 2026 instead.
