title: Test Proctoring
content:
# Test Proctoring

![](https://www.cpp.edu/admissions/img/icons/checklist_icon_blue.png)

title: Test Proctoring
content:
## Testing Information

Test Proctoring is offered to Cal Poly Pomona students who need to schedule a make-up exam **due to serious and compelling reasons** (i.e. illness). Faculty must submit a **Faculty** **Request Form** for approved make-up exams. For scheduling purposes, Faculty Request Forms must be received at least **48\* hours (2 business days)** in advance of completion date and time. Please note that students registered with the Disability Resource Center (DRC) who need additional accommodations will need to schedule their make up exam with the DRC. The DRC can be contacted at **(909) 869-3333** or **drctesting@cpp.edu**.

Testing Services now offers ALEKS PPL Assessment test procotoring for incoming freshman.

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### Test Proctoring Hours

**Monday-Friday 9:00 a.m.-12:00 p.m.**

**Monday-Friday 2:00 p.m.-4:00 p.m.**

**Once the faculty submits their request (at least 2 business days in advance of the completion date):**

* All make-up tests are available Monday-Friday, **by appointment only,** and are scheduled by Testing Services in CPP Connect at least 12-hours in advance of the student's requested appointment time, and are on a first-come, first-served basis.
* Seating per day is limited.
* Check in for test proctoring at the  [**Learning Resource Center (LRC)**](https://www.cpp.edu/lrc/ "learning resource center") in the University Library, 15-2921.
* Students registered with the [**Disability Resource Center (DRC)**](https://www.cpp.edu/drc/index.shtml "link to the drc") and needing additional accommodations will need to schedule a make-up exam with the DRC.

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### Faculty Only

* Submit a make-up exam request for your student (**2 business days\* in advance** of completion date and time). **Requests submitted by students directly** **will be denied**. **\***Requests made on Friday after 12:00 pm must have a completion date no earlier than the following Tuesday.
* Only CPP faculty can submit a request form. **CPP account sign-in is required** otherwise you will receive an error message stating that you do not have permission to access the form.
* **Please ensure allowable materials are accurate** before submitting the request form.
* Do not send any other material not pertinent to the exam, especially answer keys, when submitting exams.
* **Testing Services is not responsible for reviewing uploaded testing materials**.
* Proctoring requests for exams that require the use of multiple browsers during the exam or online resources/platforms may not be accommodated.
* Excel files and other supporting materials needed to complete exams must be provided by instructors and accessed by the students through their Canvas course during the exam.
* Uploaded test materials **must be submitted as a PDF**.
* Please refer to the Faculty FAQ for further information.

[Faculty Request Form](https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=HqZLFuw5XU-J_6ofAKUhtGVS4WQgth9HsmMxhMgBGCpUMEdRVlNQSzQ4TFY1TjhSQzNERjlUTUpMVy4u "Faculty Request Form. ")

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### Proctoring Protocol

* Students must bring a valid photo ID.
* Once the proctored exam has begun, students may not leaving the testing room.
* Electronics not specified as testing material by the instructor or department are not allowed, including but not limited to: phones, smart watches, and calculators.
* Students must adhere to the University **[Student Conduct Code](https://www.cpp.edu/studentconduct/student-conduct-code.shtml "link to student conduct and integrity")** at all times while in the testing room.

title: Test Proctoring
content:
## ALEKS Calculus Assessment Proctoring

* Students will need their Bronconame and Bronco password to access ALEKS.
* Students must follow the Proctoring Protocol outlined above.
* Students are encouraged to take and complete an ALEKS Practice Assessment before the proctored assessment. This will give you a baseline and let you know which topics you need to review.
* After taking the practice assessment, the ALEKS PPL system will give you the opportunity to use learning modules to review and improve. By spending time with ALEKS, you will be better prepared and more successful in whatever math class you take in the fall.
* Students who have started an ALEKS Practice Assessment, **MUST COMPLETE** it in its entirety before taking the proctored assessment. The system will prevent a student from taking the proctored assessment until the practice assessment is complete. Clicking "I don't know" on every problem not answered will be recommended before entering the testing room.
* Please visit the [**Mathematics and Statistics Department**](https://www.cpp.edu/sci/mathematics-statistics/calculus-placement.shtml "link to Math and Stats dept ALEKS website") for further information on the ALEKS PPL Assessment.

title: Test Proctoring
content:
## Requirements

* All test materials and instructions from faculty must be submitted **for each student** via the Faculty Request Form. **Once these materials are received**, Testing Services will reach out to the student to schedule an appointment.
* Students must present valid photo identification (Bronco ID card or other valid government ID) upon check-in to their scheduled make-up test. Students without valid ID will be denied seating for the test.
* Students should arrive to the LRC **5 minutes prior** to their scheduled appointment. Students arriving late will forfeit their appointment.
* Students who fail to arrive at their scheduled time (late or no-show) will need their faculty to submit a new request form.
* Proctors do not provide scantrons, bluebooks, calculators, or pencils and cannot help troubleshoot technical issues with electronic devices.
* For online exams use of student owned laptops are encouraged.
* For questions email **testingservices@cpp.edu**.

title: Test Proctoring
content:
## Accommodations

Students requesting accommodations for disabilities must contact the **[Disability Resource Center (DRC)](https://www.cpp.edu/~drc/)**. The DRC can be contacted at (909) 869-3333 or **drc@cpp.edu**.

title: Test Proctoring
content:
## Academic Integrity

Make-up testing will end immediately for students engaging in academic dishonesty and the instructor will be notified. Please refer to the [**Office of Student Conduct and Integrity**](https://www.cpp.edu/studentconduct/student-conduct-code.shtml "office of student conduct and integrity") for further information.

title: Test Proctoring
content: ## Student Frequently Asked Questions

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### I'm not a Cal Poly Pomona student. Can I take my exam through this service?

At this time, we only offer make-up exams for currently-enrolled Cal Poly Pomona students.
section: I'm not a Cal Poly Pomona student. Can I take my exam through this service?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### What if I show up late to my appointment?

Since the proctor cannot leave the room once the exam begins, we cannot accommodate late arrivals. If you are unable to attend the appointment at the scheduled time, notify your instructor to submit a new Faculty Request Form.
section: What if I show up late to my appointment?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### What if I need to cancel or reschedule my appointment?

Email **testingservices@cpp.edu**. If the request to reschedule is within the timeframe indicated by your instructor on the Faculty Request Form, and there is availability, Testing Services will accommodate your request to reschedule. You will receive an email confirmation from CPP Connect with the new day/time. If you do not show to your scheduled appointment or your rescheduling request cannot be accommodated, you will need your faculty to submit a new Faculty Request Form.
section: What if I need to cancel or reschedule my appointment?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### How do I request my make-up exam?

The process begins with your instructor. Your instructor must submit a Faculty Request Form. After the form is received and processed, the student will receive an email with appointment availability. Please keep in mind that appointments are scheduled on a first-come, first-served basis. Reply promptly to ensure you meet your instructor's deadline.
section: How do I request my make-up exam?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### Can I take the CCE, GWT, or PCPT exams through this service?

Testing Services currently proctors make-up exams for courses only. Please inquire with the respective academic departments directly regarding these other exams.

* CCE - Contact the Political Science Department.
* GWT - This test has been suspended.
* PCPT - Contact the Computer Information Systems Department.
section: Can I take the CCE, GWT, or PCPT exams through this service?

title: Test Proctoring
content: ## Faculty Frequently Asked Questions

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### How will I get the completed exam?

Faculty indicate on the Faculty Request Form if they want the completed exam materials scanned to PDF and emailed (**within 1 business day**), or if they will collect the physical exam materials from the Learning Resource Center (Library, 15-2921). Faculty must show ID to pick up exams. Exams will be shredded after the appropriate grade appeal date for each term.
section: How will I get the completed exam?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### How soon can I expect my student to take their make-up exam?

For scheduling purposes, Faculty Request Forms must be received **at least 48 hours in advance** of completion date and time. After you submit your request for an exam, we will do our best to accommodate your students as quickly as possible.

Seating is limited and appointments are scheduled on a first-come, first-served basis. Please make your students aware of your testing expectations and to check their email frequently for the appointment invite from **testingservices@cpp.edu**and any updates. We may not be able to accommodate same-day requests due to capacity and proctor availability.
section: How soon can I expect my student to take their make-up exam?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### My exam is administered online. What equipment and software options are provided to students?

Testing Services cannot provide technical assistance, equipment (laptops at this time), or help with installing any specialized software for purposes of taking exams. Student personal laptops are encouraged for use in these situations.

Faculty are also encouraged to clearly communicate all technical requirements on the Faculty Request Form and with students prior to them sitting for proctored exams. This includes what software needs to be installed and whether Apple or Windows PCs are preferred.
section: My exam is administered online. What equipment and software options are provided to students?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### How do I provide the exam materials to the proctors?

The materials (PDF) must be attached to the Faculty Request Form submitted. You may also contact testingservices@cpp.edu for additional assistance.
section: How do I provide the exam materials to the proctors?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### Can I submit one Facullty Request Form for multiple students?

No. Each student must have a Faculty Request Form with exam material submitted for Testing Services to proctor their exam.
section: Can I submit one Facullty Request Form for multiple students?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### What if there are changes to my exam after I submitted the Faculty Request Form?

You may email testingservices@cpp.edu with the updated exam or instructions.
section: What if there are changes to my exam after I submitted the Faculty Request Form?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
### Can the proctor grade the exam?

No, the proctors will not grade the exams. Please DO NOT send any answer keys. All test materials submitted in the Faculty Request Form will be printed and provided to the student during their appointment.
section: Can the proctor grade the exam?

title: Test Proctoring
content:
All test materials submitted in the Faculty Request Form will be printed and provided to the student during their appointment.

content:
## Programs & Workshops at the Hub

[### Feb 04 2026

Linkedin Workshop

Looking to strengthen your professional online presence and expand your career opportunities? Join this interactive LinkedIn workshop led by Cal Poly Pomona Career Coach Beth Lee, designed for community members at all career stages. Whether you are exploring new career paths, reentering the workforce, or seeking advancement, this workshop will provide practical, hands-on guidance to help you use LinkedIn effectively as a career development tool.

5:00 PM](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/linkedin-workshop-tickets-1979832709931?aff=ebdsoporgprofile)

[### Feb 11 2026

Food and Nutrition Workshop

This workshop offers an in-depth exploration of foundational food and nutrition principles, empowering participants to make informed, evidence-based decisions about their dietary habits. Through an engaging and educational format, attendees will learn how nutrition impacts physical health, energy levels, and overall well-being.

Participants will gain practical, research-informed strategies for mindful and balanced eating, including how to evaluate food choices, understand individual nutritional needs, and apply sustainable habits that support long-term wellness. Attendees are encouraged to bring their nutrition-related questions and concerns, as faculty experts Dr. Sangeeta Shrivastava and Dr. Fatheeema Begum Subhan will be available to address questions and provide guidance throughout the session.

5:00 PM](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/food-nutrition-workshop-tickets-1979937429149?aff=oddtdtcreator)

[### Mar 11 2026

Microenterprise Collaborative of Inland Southern California Workshop

Join us for an informative and engaging session led by Guadalupe Duran, Program Assistant with the Microenterprise Collaborative of Inland Southern California. This workshop is designed for aspiring and existing entrepreneurs seeking practical guidance and local support to start, grow, or stabilize their small businesses.

5:00 PM](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/microenterprise-collaborative-of-inland-southern-california-workshop-tickets-1980032454372?aff=ebdsoporgprofile)

[### Mar 25 2026

How to Turn Your Hobby Into a Business

Do you have a hobby or passion you’ve always dreamed of turning into a business? This interactive workshop is designed to help aspiring entrepreneurs explore how to transform their skills, interests, and creative ideas into a viable business opportunity.

Hosted by Natalia Amador, this session will guide participants through the early stages of entrepreneurship, including identifying market opportunities, understanding the basics of business planning, and taking practical first steps toward launching a small business.

5:30 PM](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/how-to-turn-your-hobby-into-a-business-tickets-1979953147162?aff=ebdsoporgprofile)

[Calendar of Events](https://www.eventbrite.com/o/community-innovation-hub-88957935913)

title: Empowering Pomona's Future: Innovating through Science, Technology, and Entrepreneurship at the The Hub.
content:
### The Community Innovation Hub is dedicated to invigorating Pomona's workforce through comprehensive Workforce Development initiatives.

This center plays a pivotal role in:

* Enhancing Skills: By offering a range of programs focused on upskilling and reskilling, the Hub equips community members with the necessary tools and knowledge to thrive in various STEAM disciplines.

* Empowering Entrepreneurs and Local Businesses: The Hub serves as a resourceful beacon for small businesses and entrepreneurs, providing access to essential services, co-working spaces, and equipment for innovation and growth.

* Strengthening Community Ties: In response to the significant employment challenges exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic, the Community Innovation Hub stands as a cornerstone for economic revival, fostering a skilled, diverse workforce that supports and sustains local business ecosystems.

![](img/photos/homepage/about.jpeg)

![](img/banners/homepage/cihbanner_news0.jpg)

content:
## University Receives $1M for Community STEA2M Innovation Hub CPP News

It's full steam ahead for Cal Poly Pomona's Bronco STEA2M Innovation Hub, after a $1 million federal funding request by U.S. Rep. Norma Torres was approved in a sweeping 2022 appropriations bill signed earlier this month by President Joe Biden. The project - an entrepreneurial and small business hub that will provide workforce training programs and a full complement of workspaces and fabrication equipment - was folded into a $14.3 million request to invest in 10 Inland Empire community programs. The hub is slated to open shop in downtown Pomona. Its location will be announced at a later date.

[Read more on CPP News](https://www.cpp.edu/news/content/2022/03/university-receives-1m-for-community-stea2m-innovation-hub/index.shtml "Read more on CPP News. ")

![](img/photos/news/ilab-tour-ceis.jpeg)

title: About The Hub
content:
## Background

In 2022, the [Office of Academic Innovation](https://www.cpp.edu/ai/index.shtml) secured a [$1 million grant](https://polycentric.cpp.edu/2022/03/university-receives-1m-for-community-stea2m-innovation-hub/) requested by U.S. Representative Norma Torres as part of a $14.3 million appeal to invest in 10 Inland Empire community programs. The grant period covers four years of lease and operating expenses for an entrepreneurial and small business hub, with a spend-by date of September 30, 2027.

The center, initially called the Bronco STEA2M Innovation Hub, will offer programming in science, technology, engineering, education, art, agriculture, and math to reskill and upskill members of the Pomona community. It will also serve as a business resource and development center for small businesses and entrepreneurs.

Employment in Pomona was hit especially hard by the COVID-19 pandemic. The unemployment rate in Pomona increased from 4.9% to 14.7% in January 2021. While the community is continuing to rebound, Cal Poly Pomona will provide this space to educate community members, support local entrepreneurs and businesses, and develop a skilled, diverse workforce pipeline.

![](../img/photos/about-us/about-us.jpeg)

![](../img/banners/about-us/cihbanner_missionvision0.jpg)

title: About The Hub
content:
## Our Mission

---

![](../img/icons/about-us/wired-flat-489-rocket-space.gif)

title: About The Hub
content:
### Our mission is to establish and sustain an ecosystem that fosters creativity, innovation, and entrepreneurship within the Pomona and Cal Poly Pomona communities. We are committed to cultivating the success of a diverse local community by:

1. Supporting workforce and community development goals, including upskilling and reskilling, through entrepreneurship programming.
2. Promoting social impact and awareness of inequality through education. We empower individuals in the Pomona and Cal Poly Pomona communities to utilize their voices effectively, equipping them with tools for problem-solving and engaging them in meaningful social and civic actions.
3. Leveraging Cal Poly Pomona’s polytechnic advantage to offer innovation programming in STEAAM disciplines. This initiative aims to enhance core competencies and provide upskilling opportunities in science, technology, engineering, agriculture, arts, and math, benefiting both the university and the broader Pomona community.
4. Providing entrepreneurs, startups, small and family-owned businesses, and community members in Pomona and Cal Poly Pomona easier access to co-working spaces and fabrication equipment.
5. Connecting entrepreneurs and businesses in the Pomona area with collaboration opportunities with local universities. This includes access to talent (e.g. internships, startup employees), technology (e.g. research collaboration with faculty and labs), and federal grant opportunities and partnerships (e.g. SBIR, NSF I-Corps Program).
6. Creating a network of service providers, including lawyers, accountants, and consultants, to offer essential support to entrepreneurs, startups, and small family-owned businesses.
7. Providing networking opportunities within the local community and connecting to broader entrepreneurial ecosystems to enhance collaboration and growth. Establishing connections with funders, investors, and advisors to provide essential financial guidance and opportunities for businesses at various stages of development.

Through these efforts, the Community Innovation Hub aims to make a significant impact in both the Pomona and Cal Poly Pomona communities, fostering a culture of lifelong learning and societal advancement.
section: Our mission is to establish and sustain an ecosystem that fosters creativity, innovation, and entrepreneurship within the Pomona and Cal Poly Pomona communities. We are committed to cultivating the success of a diverse local community by:

title: About The Hub
content:
## Our Vision

---

![](../img/icons/about-us/wired-flat-69-eye.gif)

Our vision is to be a transformative hub in the Pomona and Cal Poly Pomona communities, fostering a vibrant culture of innovation, entrepreneurship, and societal responsibility. We strive to be a leading catalyst for change, where creative minds from diverse backgrounds converge to develop solutions for contemporary challenges. Our goal is to inspire and equip small businesses with the skills, resources, and networks needed to drive sustainable progress, empowering them to make a positive impact on our local community, region, and world.

About

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### About

×

Chará Swodeck is a community collaborator, problem solver, and innovator; bringing ideas to life with goals, milestones, and conditions of satisfaction. Owner of Open Box Designs, LLC - Design and Marketing Consultants - Chará has spent over 20 years in event production and design, and 6 award-winning years in television/video production. She currently serves as Executive Board Member of the Fairplex Child Development Center, Founding President & CEO of the African American Advisory Alliance (Pomona4As), Pomona Girl Scouts Service Unit Manager, previous Chair of City of Pomona Historic Preservation Commission, VP of Communication for National Women’s Political Caucus - Pomona / Inland Empire (NWPC-PIE), Founder of Kwanzaa Joy Celebration and Shop Downtown Pomona. Chará Swodeck has served as President of the Pomona Council Parent Teacher Association (PTA), Volunteer Coordinator for the Pomona Christmas Parade for 11 years, Coordinator of the annual superhero-themed Pomona Children’s Festival, and much more. Connecting the dots, designing events, and creating impactful experiences have been part of who she is since she was a child. Chará Swodeck and her family have been committed to not only giving back, but also engaging in community building and advocating for equitable policy for her neighborhoods in art, business, education, housing, and public safety. Along with her husband, Joshua, they have lived in Pomona for 21 years and have two children.

Close

About

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board

### About

×

Andrea Rico is the Governmental and Community Affairs Manager for the City of Pomona. She has worked for Pomona for 17 years. Ms. Rico serves as the City’s liaison to community-based initiatives and efforts. Her focus in incorporating community voice into decision making through authentic community engagement. Currently, she is the City’s dedicated staff member dedicated to Pomona’s Investment in the Early Childhood Ecosystem (PIECE). In addition, she also oversees the Pomona Household Universal Grant (HUG) Pilot Program which is a guaranteed income program for parents or guardians prenatal to three.

Prior to her current position, she worked for the City of Santa Ana as an Educational Program Coordinator for eight years and was responsible for at-risk and prevention programs for youth and their families. She has over 25 years of experience working in the field of community engagement and programs. Mrs. Rico holds a Bachelor’s Degree in Psychology from Vanguard University and a Master’s Degree from Chapman University in Criminal Justice with an emphasis on behavior.

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### Hector Lopez

#### Programming Committee Co-Chair

##### Managing Director | RE/MAX Commercial

About

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### About

×

Hector Lopez grew up in Chino Hills and Los Angeles in a family of 8. As the eldest, he was very closely involved in the family business of food service distribution in downtown LA. Both his mother and father immigrated to the United States in their very early twenties in pursuit of the American Dream.

Professionally, Hector Lopez is a seasoned Commercial Real Estate Agent with a focus on industrial and mixed-use properties. With over 16 years of experience in CRE, Hector has honed his expertise in helping property owners and prospective buyers achieve their long-term real estate goals. His primary focus has been in the Pomona and the surrounding markets. He is currently the Managing Director at the REMAX Commercial in Chino Hills, CA.

Growing up in an immigrant owed small business has given him a great deal of perspective of the difficulties and challenges of owning a small business in California. Often times the business owner is the sales team, human resources, accountant and even delivery driver all at the same time.

“Seeing the struggles of owning a small business has helped me understand what the individual usually needs. I strive to help them achieve the goals they want and often times helping to open their eyes to the possibility of where the next goal should be. Seeing someone grow from a one-man operation to a full blow 10 or even 100+ employee business brings me great professional fulfillment.” -Hector Lopez Jr

He was introduced to Pomona in my early 20s when he met his now wife, Jessica. Pomona would typically only be known for auto repair or taco-related reasons. Meeting Jessica gave him more motivation to be in Pomona. Fast forward through their wedding and 2 kids as of 2024, they are still happy residents of Pomona.

“Living and working in Pomona has opened my eyes to some amazing people in this city. There is a lot of help needed to continue to push residents and business residents further down the road to success. I volunteer my time to Co-Chair the Economic Development Initiative as well as sit on the Board of Pomona’s Promise. My ultimate goal is to help everyone I come in contact with from making a simple business card to helping them purchase their first building.” -Hector Lopez Jr

Close

![Matt Ryczek](../img/photos/contact-us/matt-ryczek.jpg)

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### Matt Ryczek

#### Sustainability and Fund Development Committee Chair

##### Chief Financial Officer | Idea House & Co.

About

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### About

×

Matt Ryczek’s career is defined by resilience, resourcefulness, and an unwavering belief that every challenge presents a path forward. With over 20 years of experience in product launch strategy, engineering, design, brand development, and finance, Matt has become a trusted leader in guiding new product inventions from concept to market. His involvement across public, non-profit, and private sectors, where he has held multiple board positions, highlights his broad-reaching impact and strategic insight.

Matt’s career spans all aspects of product development, from hands-on roles in engineering and design to leadership positions in project management and strategy. His journey, supported by his MBA from Concordia University, has given him a unique perspective on bringing ideas to life. He holds numerous US patents and has worked with distinguished brands like Walt Disney Imagineering, Verizon, Behr Paints, Home Depot, and TRX Training, while also supporting many growing brands.

For the past five years, Matt has served as the CFO of Idea House & Co., where his role extends beyond traditional finance. He is the architect behind the company’s growth and the lead strategist for clients, helping them navigate the complexities of product launches. Under his guidance, Idea House has transformed into a client-focused, data-driven organization that prioritizes transparency, accurate forecasting, and meaningful growth.

Matt’s ability to reshape Idea House’s operations—from restructuring internal processes to expanding the team—demonstrates his creative and strategic approach to leadership. His expertise enables him to help businesses not just launch products, but to create solutions that matter.

Outside of work, Matt finds balance in activities like hiking, playing sports, camping, or setting off on new adventures. These moments of connection and exploration reflect his belief in maintaining a well-rounded life, both personally and professionally.

At the core of Matt’s philosophy is the belief that good ideas deserve a path forward. His guiding principle is simple: Do what you can with what you have now. Matt’s vision for Idea House & Co. is to continue being a trusted voice in product development, dedicated to nurturing innovative ideas and pushing industries forward.

Close

![Monique Robles](../img/photos/contact-us/momo.jpg)

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### Monique Robles

#### Programming Committee Co-Chair & Business Support Liaison

##### President & CEO | Pomona Chamber of Commerce

About

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### About

×

Monique Robles was born in 1978 and raised in Pomona, California. Monique struggled in  her late teens and early 20’s as a single mother, uncertain of her future. At the young age of  21, she embarked on a career as a Correctional Officer. However, her journey took an  unexpected turn in 2008 when she was medically retired due to an on-the-job injury.

Determined to make the most of her situation, Monique pursued a bachelor's degree in  human services. With her newfound knowledge and passion for helping others, Monique  ventured into the world of nonprofit management, accumulating over 20 years of  experience in the field. Throughout her career, she has made significant contributions to  various organizations.

Currently, Monique is actively involved in several prestigious positions within numerous  community organizations and specialized committees. She Co-Chairs the Economic Development Committee for Pomona’s Promise and the College, Career and Workforce  Development Taskforce- LAP3. She is a member of the Business Engagement Committee  for the Economic Development Opportunity Department for Los Angeles County, the Cal  Poly Innovation Hub Advisory Committee, The Fox Community Cultural Foundation, and  the Pomona Unified School District Grant Oversite Committee. And most recently, was  asked to be a part of the Leadership Team for the City of Pomona Conrad-Hilton Grant.

As a visionary business leader, she strives to foster a strong sense of community and  inclusion among businesses and all individuals within the city of Pomona. In her current  role as President and CEO of the Pomona Chamber of Commerce, Monique is fully  committed to supporting the efforts of the city's leadership and the community to facilitate  the growth and prosperity of Pomona.

Close

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### Dr. Olukemi Sawyerr

#### Ex Officio Board Member

##### Associate Vice President, Office of Academic Innovation | Cal Poly Pomona

About

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### About

×

Olukemi Sawyerr, Ph.D., is the Inaugural associate vice president for Academic Innovation at California State Polytechnic University, Pomona (Cal Poly Pomona) where she provides strategic direction and administrative oversight to the [Office of Academic Innovation](https://www.cpp.edu/ai/index.shtml), which curates opportunities for experiential learning, discovery, and innovation for the Cal Poly Pomona community, through the [Career Center](https://www.cpp.edu/career/index.shtml),  [Center for Community Engagement](https://www.cpp.edu/cce/), [Kellogg Honors College](https://www.cpp.edu/honorscollege/index.shtml), [Innovation Incubator](https://www.cpp.edu/ii/index.shtml), [Office of Undergraduate Research](https://www.cpp.edu/our-cpp/), and the [Student Innovation Idea Labs](https://www.cpp.edu/siil/). She is the Principal Investigator (PI) on [CPP INVESTS](https://www.cpp.edu/cpp-invests/index.shtml), a $3 million National Science Foundation (NSF) HSI Improving Undergraduate STEM Education grant, and the [CPP NSF I-Corps site](https://www.cpp.edu/i-corps/index.shtml) grant. She is PI on a Small Business Administration $1 million grant supporting the creation of the Community Innovation Hub in the city of Pomona CA, funding that was secured for Cal Poly Pomona through the office of Congresswoman Norma Torres. She is also the PI on a Michael and Susan Dell Foundation grant of $1.6 million and a California Community Foundation Grant of $100,000 both supporting course-embedded micro-internship in non-profit organizations to provide students professional experiences to ready them for their future life. She has also served as PI on multiple grants aimed at supporting innovation and entrepreneurship in the Pomona Valley Region.

Dr. Sawyerr is leading the development and implementation of [Future Career Paths](https://www.cpp.edu/cpp-invests/career.shtml), a career and professional readiness model to prepare Cal Poly Pomona students for the future of work and human and civic engagement. Before this role, she served as the inaugural director of the Student Innovation Idea Lab (iLab), creating the infrastructure to engender and support student creativity, innovation, and entrepreneurship. She has overseen the creation of innovative learning spaces on campus including the Maker Studio on the second floor of the Library and Innovation Orchard at Ganesha High School; and founded multiple innovative programs including programs in partnership with NASA Armstrong Flight Research Center. Her curricular innovations have garnered her national and international attention, including invited presentations at the Thematic University-Business Forum: Breaking Boundaries for Future Careers sponsored by the European Commission, and at the International Forum on Industry and Education sponsored by the Center for Schooling Development Programme in China.

Her current research interests focus on entrepreneurship education and its impact in higher education. Her research has appeared in multiple peer-reviewed journals and she has presented her work at national and international academic and professional conferences. Dr. Sawyerr received her Ph.D. in Strategic Management from the University of North Texas and her Bachelor of Business Administration and MBA from Texas Woman’s University. She is a professor of strategy and entrepreneurship in the Management and Human Resources Department in the College of Business Administration at Cal Poly Pomona.

Close

![Ericka Olguin](../img/photos/contact-us/ericka-olguin.jpg)

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### Ericka Olguin

#### Ex-Officio Board Member

##### Cal Poly Pomona

About

title: Innovation Hub Advisory Board
content:
### About

×

Ericka Olguin is the Director of the Innovation Incubator and Interim Director of the Center for Community Engagement at California State Polytechnic University, Pomona (Cal Poly Pomona), where she advances a culture of innovation in teaching, learning, and community partnerships. She leads the design and implementation of initiatives such as micro-internships, digital credentialing, engaged learning, and the university’s signature PolyX program—all of which expand opportunities for student success through discovery, experiential learning, and innovation.

 A proud Cal Poly Pomona alumna, Ericka holds a Master of Business Administration (MBA) and a Bachelor of Arts in Ethnic and Women’s Studies. With more than 25 years of experience in education, she has dedicated her career to advancing diversity, equity, and inclusion by developing programs that close opportunity gaps for underrepresented students and communities.

Her leadership includes creating and scaling high-impact programs and spaces such as the Micro-Internship Program (embedding real-world projects into coursework), the Digital Credentialing & Badging initiative (with more than 15,000 badges awarded), and the PolyX Hub (a repository connecting students to signature polytechnic experiences). She also launched innovation spaces like the Cal Poly Pomona Makers Studio in the University Library, the Innovation Orchard in Pomona, and the Student Innovation Idea Lab on campus. Additionally, she developed and led entrepreneurship initiatives including iStartup Academy, 3-Day Startup (Pomona Innovation Weekend), Women’s Entrepreneurship Day, and the Jr. Entrepreneurship Camp, which continues to inspire young innovators today.

Known for her unifying presence, Ericka creates spaces where people feel seen, heard, and valued—bringing communities together through empathy, dialogue, and respect. At the Community Innovation Hub, she is committed to strengthening connections between the university and the Pomona community, fostering collaboration that drives creativity, equity, and impact.

Close

# The Hub in the News

![Students at Dia de los muertos altar](img/photos/news/cpp-latinx-heritage-month.jpg)

title: The Hub in the News
content:
## CPP Recognizes Latinx Heritage Month

Cal Poly Pomona celebrates Latinx Heritage Month and Hispanic-Serving Institutions Week with a variety of events. From a luncheon to a panel discussion to fun activities, campus organizations and resource centers from Sept. 15 to Oct. 15 will highlight and honor the contributions of the Latinx community, as well as acknowledge the history and culture of more than 20 Latin American countries. The entire Bronco community is welcome to participate in these events, regardless of identity.

[Read more about CPP Recognizes Latinx Heritage Month](https://www.cpp.edu/news/content/2025/09/cpp-recognizes-latinx-heritage-month/index.shtml)

![panelists at cafe con conversaciones](img/photos/news/community-innovation-hub-news-2.jpg)

title: The Hub in the News
content:
## Community Innovation Hub Uplifts Latinx Voices for Latinx Heritage Month

Cal Poly Pomona’s [Community Innovation Hub](https://cpp.edu/thehub) in Downtown Pomona welcomed a crowd of over 40 community members on Sept. 17 for Cafe y Conversaciones, an engaging event highlighting Latinx Heritage Month. Seven Latinx entrepreneurs and community leaders shared their experiences, insights, and reflections on the impact of small businesses and the role of cultural identity in shaping entrepreneurship.

[Read more about Community Innovation Hub Uplifts Latinx Voices for Latinx Heritage Month](https://www.cpp.edu/news/content/2025/09/community-innovation-hub-uplifts-latinx-voices-for-latinx-heritage-month/index.shtml)

title: Programs & Workshops
content: # Programs & Workshops

title: Programs & Workshops
content:
## Events Calendar

[### Feb 04 2026

Linkedin Workshop

Looking to strengthen your professional online presence and expand your career opportunities? Join this interactive LinkedIn workshop led by Cal Poly Pomona Career Coach Beth Lee, designed for community members at all career stages. Whether you are exploring new career paths, reentering the workforce, or seeking advancement, this workshop will provide practical, hands-on guidance to help you use LinkedIn effectively as a career development tool.

5:00 PM](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/linkedin-workshop-tickets-1979832709931?aff=ebdsoporgprofile)

[### Feb 11 2026

Food and Nutrition Workshop

This workshop offers an in-depth exploration of foundational food and nutrition principles, empowering participants to make informed, evidence-based decisions about their dietary habits. Through an engaging and educational format, attendees will learn how nutrition influences physical health, energy levels, and overall well-being.

Participants will gain practical, research-informed strategies for mindful and balanced eating, including how to evaluate food choices, understand individual nutritional needs, and apply sustainable habits that support long-term wellness. By the end of the workshop, participants will be equipped to identify credible nutrition information, make informed dietary decisions, and incorporate mindful eating practices into their daily lives.

5:00 PM](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/food-nutrition-workshop-tickets-1979937429149?aff=oddtdtcreator)

[### Mar 11 2026

Microenterprise Collaborative of Inland Southern California Workshop

Join us for an informative and engaging session led by Guadalupe Duran, Program Assistant with the Microenterprise Collaborative of Inland Southern California. This workshop is designed for aspiring and existing entrepreneurs seeking practical guidance and local support to start, grow, or stabilize their small businesses.

5:00 PM](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/microenterprise-collaborative-of-inland-southern-california-workshop-tickets-1980032454372?aff=ebdsoporgprofile)

[### Mar 25 2026

How to Turn Your Hobby Into a Business

Do you have a hobby or passion you’ve always dreamed of turning into a business? This interactive workshop is designed to help aspiring entrepreneurs explore how to transform their skills, interests, and creative ideas into a viable business opportunity.

Hosted by Natalia Amador, this session will guide participants through the early stages of entrepreneurship, including identifying market opportunities, understanding the basics of business planning, and taking practical first steps toward launching a small business.

5:30 PM](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/how-to-turn-your-hobby-into-a-business-tickets-1979953147162?aff=ebdsoporgprofile)

[Calendar of Events](https://www.eventbrite.com/o/community-innovation-hub-88957935913)

# Community Innovation Hub Reservations

title: Community Innovation Hub Reservations
content:
## Create. Collaborate. Thrive. Where community and innovation meet.

Located in the heart of downtown Pomona, the Community Innovation Hub at [101 W. Mission Blvd., Suite 111](https://www.google.com/maps?ll=34.055612,-117.750482&z=15&t=m&hl=en&gl=US&mapclient=embed&q=101+W+Mission+Blvd+%23111+Pomona,+CA+91766), is a welcoming space designed to inspire collaboration, creativity, and community impact. Whether you're hosting a workshop, meeting, or event, we’re here to support your vision.

Please note: Submitting this form is a **request only** and does **not** guarantee a reservation. A member of our Hub staff will review your submission and respond within **2–3 business days** to confirm availability or request additional information.

We appreciate your interest and look forward to helping you bring your ideas to life.

[Make a Reservation](https://app.smartsheet.com/b/form/ff8cca272a794e91803e84cc090da129 "Make a Reservation. ")

title: AQMD Survey
content:
# AQMD Survey

All State faculty, staff, student assistants (who work more than 4 or more hours this week only), Foundation, and ASI employees at Cal Poly Pomona are required to complete an Employee Commute Survey.  The survey keeps the campus in compliance with Rule 2202 of the South Coast Air Quality Management District.  This survey tracks how employees travel to campus over a five-day (Monday through Friday) period.  The survey yields an Average Vehicle Ridership number and measures the effectiveness of Cal Poly Pomona’s Rideshare Program.

The survey takes approximately 2 minutes to complete.

If you have any questions or comments about the survey please contact your department representative or rideshare@cpp.edu. This survey must be completed even if you are not currently in the Rideshare Program. If you would like to know more about the Rideshare Program please contact Dave Flores.

Thank you for your participation. Your feedback will assist us in improving our campus Rideshare Program and incentives.

title: Foothill Transit Class Pass
content: # Foothill Transit Class Pass

title: Foothill Transit Class Pass
content: ### ![Free unlimited rides with foothill transit](img/class-pass-banner.png)

title: Foothill Transit Class Pass
content:
## Students at Cal Poly Pomona get FREE RIDES on Foothill Transit Buses

Welcome to Cal Poly Pomona! As a Bronco, you get unlimited FREE RIDES on Foothill Transit buses with Class Pass. Foothill Transit Lines 190,194,195, 289, 295, 480, 482, 486, and Silver Streak all serve the Cal Poly Pomona campus – so you can get to class, downtown LA, or anywhere in between for free!

**Download the TAP App to get your Class Pass today!**

* **Get your eligibility code** that you can find in your [student portal.](https://my.cpp.edu/uPortal/f/home/normal/render.uP) You'll see it on the top right corner of the 'Student Life' tab. If accessing your portal via phone, use the search bar to search for Foothill Transit and select the first option that appears, *Foothill Transit Class Pass.*Keep this code handy throughout the process.
* **Download the app.** Search “TAP LA” on the App Store or Play Store.
* **Create an account.** Follow the prompts on the app to create your account.
* **Click the Cards section to add a TAP card.** Click the icon at the top of the screen then select:
  + **Add a TAP Card to Apple Wallet (iPhone)**
  + **Add a TAP Card to my phone (Android)**
* **Enter your code.** In the app, select “Add Stored Value or Pass” then select “Redeem Code”. Next, enter your eligibility code that you accessed in your student portal.
* **Claim your Class Pass.** The Class Pass will be added to your cart – don’t worry it’ll be free. Press “purchase” and you’re all set to ride! Make sure to use your pass within two weeks to activate it.

Need more help? Click [here](https://www.taptogo.net/Fareless/articles/en_US/GoPass_article/Add-your-GoPass-TAP-Card-to-your-Phone) for more information using the TAP App to load your pass.

Make sure to use your pass on the bus within two weeks of registering online to activate your pass for the school year.

Don’t have a smartphone? Don’t worry. Stop by the BRIC to pick up a TAP card and check your eligibility. Make sure to note down your eligibility code from your student portal, too. You’ll need that to sign up. Then, visit taptogo.net/calpolypomonaclasspass and complete the prompts. Learn more at [foothilltransit.org/cpp](https://www.foothilltransit.org/cpp).

***NEW!* Ride Foothill Transit Bus Line 295 from the San Dimas Metro A-Line station straight to Cal Poly Pomona.**

**Directions to Bus Stop:**

**When exiting the San Dimas station, walk west towards San Dimas Ave., turn right on San Dimas Ave. and continue walking until you reach Bonita Ave. The Line 295 bus stop is located just steps from the corner of San Dimas Ave. and Bonita Ave.**
section: Students at Cal Poly Pomona get FREE RIDES on Foothill Transit Buses

title: Foothill Transit Class Pass
content:
### Who is Eligible for the Class Pass?

All currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students with at least 1 credit unit at Cal Poly Pomona are eligible to get a Class Pass. **Continuing Education Unit (CEU) Online Learning, CPGE, and Certificate enrolled students are not eligible at this time.**
section: Who is Eligible for the Class Pass?

title: Foothill Transit Class Pass
content:
### What's a Class Pass?

Class Pass is a program that gives current students unlimited rides on Foothill Transit buses all school year long. It also helps the entire community by reducing traffic and parking congestion around campus. Not bad for a piece of plastic.

The Class Pass is a digital card (TAP card) that is accepted on all Foothill Transit Local and Silver Streak buses, which connect you to 22 different cities throughout the San Gabriel Valley and downtown Los Angeles. Your Class Pass does not include the Commuter Express Lines 490, 493, 495, 498, 499 and 699.

Your class pass is loaded onto a TAP card that is accepted on 26 transit agencies across LA County. If you want to transfer to another agency, you can load cash value onto your card.

title: Foothill Transit Class Pass
content:
### How Do I Get My Class Pass?

**Download the TAP App to get your Class Pass today!**

1. **Download the app.** Search “TAP LA” on the App Store or Play Store.
2. **Create an account.** Follow the prompts on the app to create your account.
3. **Click the Cards section to add a TAP card.** Click the icon at the top of the screen then select
   1. **Add a TAP Card to Apple Wallet (iPhone)**
   2. **Add a TAP Card to my phone (Android)**
4. **Enter your code.** In the app, select “Add Stored Value or Pass” then select “Redeem Code”. Next, enter your eligibility code that you can find in your student portal.
5. **Claim your Class Pass.** The Class Pass will be added to your cart – don’t worry it’ll be free. Press “purchase” and you’re all set to ride! Make sure to use your pass within two weeks to activate it.

Need more help? Click [here](https://www.taptogo.net/Fareless/articles/en_US/GoPass_article/Add-your-GoPass-TAP-Card-to-your-Phone) for more information using the TAP App to load your pass.

Make sure to use your pass on the bus within two weeks of registering online to activate your pass for the school year.

Don’t have a smartphone? Don’t worry. Stop by the BRIC to pick up a TAP card and check your eligibility. Make sure to note down your eligibility code from your student portal, too. You’ll need that to sign up.
section: How Do I Get My Class Pass?

title: Foothill Transit Class Pass
content:
### Will my Class Pass still be valid over summer?

Yes! The Class Pass is valid through the start of the next school year. You don't need to be enrolled in summer courses.
section: Will my Class Pass still be valid over summer?

Foothill Transit Lines 190, 194, 195, 289, 295, 480, 482, and 486 travel throughout campus, with stops along Campus Dr and Temple Ave — check out the map for locations of stops.

title: Foothill Transit Class Pass
content:
### Is the Class Pass accepted on the Silver2Silver program?

Yes. You can use Class Pass on the Metro Silver Line if you board between El Monte and downtown Los Angeles/STAPLES Center. Visit [this website](https://nam11.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=http%3A%2F%2Ffoothilltransit.org%2Flines-and-schedules%2Fsilver2silver%2F&data=04%7C01%7Cdssedillo%40cpp.edu%7C0c60ba11fa0544b4823908d96e29700f%7C164ba61e39ec4f5d89ffaa1f00a521b4%7C0%7C0%7C637661946612683905%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C1000&sdata=YY4XUAhDGP54zCWPeg9rRESKvYf65Ti790syP7lKyQA%3D&reserved=0) for more information on the Silver2Silver partnership.

Note: If you board the Metro Silver Line outside of these locations, you will had to pay the faire on the Metro Silver Line.
section: Is the Class Pass accepted on the Silver2Silver program?

title: Foothill Transit Class Pass
content:
### What If I Don't Know How to Ride the Bus?

1. Use [Google Transit](http://maps.google.com/landing/transit/index.html) or the [Trip Planner](http://foothilltransit.org/trip-tools/trip-planner/ "Trip Planner") at the top of our website to find out what bus line you need and where your nearest bus stop is located — then check [NEXTRIP](http://foothilltransit.org/trip-tools/nextrip/) for real-time updates that show exactly when your bus will arrive.
2. Go to your bus stop — it’ll be marked with a large sign in Foothill Transit blue and green.3. Make sure you board the right bus — when a bus pulls up to the stop, look at its line number and destination on the sign above the windshield.
3. Pay your fare — just touch your Class Pass to the “TAP” symbol right on the farebox and listen for the beep.
4. Move as far to the back as you can, and find a seat or hold onto the handrail — please leave seats at the front open for elderly and disabled passengers.
5. Request a stop — when the bus approaches your stop, pull the cord or press the tape running along the windows, then exit through the doors in the back.

Still have questions? Check out [Bus Basics](http://www.foothilltransit.org/trip-tools/bus-basics/), call at 800-RIDE-INFO (743-3463), or visit our the Foothill Transit website at [foothilltransit.org](http://foothilltransit.org/) anytime!
section: What If I Don't Know How to Ride the Bus?

The Class Pass pilot program has you covered for the whole school year as long as you’re enrolled in classes! If you have any issues with you pass, visit [foothilltransit.org/cpp](https://www.foothilltransit.org/cpp).

title: Commute Choices
content:
### There are many ways to get to and from Cal Poly Pomona! Check out options below, and reach out to rideshare@cpp.edu with questions.

![Students hold up a card for Foothill Transit Class Pass](../img/students_card2.jpg)
section: There are many ways to get to and from Cal Poly Pomona! Check out options below, and reach out to rideshare@cpp.edu with questions.

title: Commute Choices
content:
## Class Pass

All eligible CPP students can get a free Foothill Transit Bus Pass to get to CPP and beyond. Bus stops are located at the south end of the campus with an easy connection to the Bronco Express shuttle.

[Get your Free Class Pass](../classpass.shtml)

![](../img/three.png)

title: Commute Choices
content:
## Carpooling

Finding a carpool partner can be quick and easy with help from the Rideshare office. If you share the ride, you can drive in the carpool lane, earn Bronco Bucks, and get preferred parking.

![](../img/one.png)

title: Commute Choices
content:
## Vanpool

Cal Poly Pomona vanpools hold 7-12 people who share the commute to the campus.  Vanpools set their own route, operating schedule, and pick-up/ drop-off location. Fares are determined by the number of passengers riding in the van, the monthly mileage, and fuel charges. Drivers may also qualify for a free fare. All vanpool riders must complete a Passenger Agreement or Driver Agreement from the Rideshare Office before they ride the van.

Foundation and ASI employees are eligible to ride vanpools.  ***Students are not eligible for the vanpool program***.  Contact the Rideshare office for available seats on vanpools.

![Foothill Transit Bus driver on a route](../img/nine.png)

title: Commute Choices
content:
## Take the Bus

Take Foothill Transit or Metro buses to campus. Reimbursements up to $80 a month are paid to rideshare participants who ride the bus. Bus stops are located a the south end of the campus with an easy connection to the Bronco Express shuttle.

![Metrolink Shuttle stop](../img/metrolink-pomona-north3.jpg)

title: Commute Choices
content:
## Take the Train

Metrolink is a great way to commute to CPP. The FREE Metrolink Connect shuttle can bring you from Pomona North Metrolink Station to campus.

![Someone riding a bike](../img/seven.png)

title: Commute Choices
content:
## Bike or Walk

Riding your bike or walking is a great way to get exercise! There are a number of bicycle racks located throughout the campus and a locked storage area in Parking Structure II. Bike maps, repair kits, and a bicycle pump are available from the Rideshare office.

title: Bicycling and Walking to School
content:
# Bicycling and Walking to School

Bicycling and Walking are cost effective and environmentally conscious ways to get to and around campus. It is important to be aware of your surroundings at all times for your own safety as well as the safety of those around you. Please obey all traffic laws and operate your bike or mobility devices at decreased speed.

![Parking structure 2 bicycle cage](../img/bikecage1.jpg)

title: Bicycling and Walking to School
content:
## Bike Cage in Parking Structure 2

Cal Poly Pomona students and staff are eligible to park their bicycle in a secure bike cage located in Parking Structure 2.  You must have a valid Bronco ID to use this service.  24 hour access to the bike cage is free.  To set-up access, please visit the Rideshare Office.

title: Bicycling and Walking to School
content:
## Bicycle Rules

Bicycles must be parked in bicycle racks provided throughout the campus. No bicycle, moped or
two-wheeled vehicles may be parked or brought inside a classroom or building, except as
expressly stated in student housing license agreements. The use of bicycles is allowed in the
following areas only in accordance with the California Vehicle Code:

1. All campus roadways and highways
2. Pathways specifically designated for bicycles
3. Campus parking lots (to access roads and pathways only)
4. Campus sidewalks with a width of 10 feet or more not designated as pedestrian only
   zones

title: Bicycling and Walking to School
content:
## Rollerblades, Skateboards, Motorized Skateboards, Roller-Skates or Similar Coasting Devices

The use of rollerblades, skateboards, motorized skateboards, roller-skates or similar coasting
devices is allowed in the following areas:

1. Walkways and sidewalks not designated as pedestrian only zones
2. Pathways specifically designated for bicycles
3. Campus parking lots to access walkways and pathways

The use of rollerblades, skateboards, motorized skateboards, roller-skates or similar coasting
devices is prohibited in all areas not listed above including but not limited to:

1. All campus roadways and highways
2. Equestrian trails and recreational trails
3. All pedestrian zones indicated by a red sign stating PEDESTRIAN ZONE ONLY
4. Drainage ditches, low walls, statues, railings
section: Rollerblades, Skateboards, Motorized Skateboards, Roller-Skates or Similar Coasting Devices

title: Bicycling and Walking to School
content:
## Report Safety Concerns

Report dangerous incidents and traffic safety concerns immediately to the University Police Department at (909) 869-3070 or dial 9-1-1 for police or medical emergencies.

Report concerns with sidewalks, signage, bike paths, pedestrian paths, line of sight, landscaping to Parking & Transportation Services at parking@cpp.edu or (909) 869-3061.

title: Bicycling and Walking to School
content:
## Lock It or Lose It!

The best way to keep your bike and mobility devices safe from thieves is to lock it properly. Lock the frame with a sturdy burly chain or U-Lock, and secure the wheels (or other quick-release components) with your lock will ensure your bike is just the way you left it. Cable locks can be cut by theives.

title: Bicycling and Walking to School
content:
## Bicycle Safety Tips when Riding

* **Intersections** - take extra caution and pay attention to cross traffic and turning vehicles.
* **Right Hook** - be wary of right turning cars in front of you.
* **Door Zone** - give yourself enough distance between you and parked cars to avoid suddenly opening doors, even when you are riding in the bike lane.
* **Storm Gates and Gutters** - both can cause you to fall.  Avoid grates that can grab your tire and gutters full of debris.
* **Potholes and Sharp Objects** - avoid these because they can cause flats.
* **Rail Crossings** - the tracks can catch your front wheel and are slippery when wet. The safest way to approach rail crossings is at a 90 degree angle.
* **Riding at Night** - when riding at night you want to make sure others can see you. State law (CVC 21201) requires that you have a white front light and red reflector on the rear. Lights that blink increase your visibility and save batteries.

title: Bicycling and Walking to School
content:
### Route Planning

Pick your bike route, calculate the total mileage, how many calories you will burn, and topography information before you take your ride on your bike!
<http://www.gmap-pedometer.com/>

title: Bicycling and Walking to School
content:
### Route Planning Instructions

In the “jump to” box, enter your starting address and click “go.” Then, on the left menu, click on “start recording.” From here, you can begin to plot your route by double-clicking anywhere on the map. It’s best to map/click in small segments, because if you create a long distance between points, the map will find the shortest distance between the two points and won’t necessarily follow your particular route. You can save your route by clicking on “save route,” and that will generate a URL specific to your data that you can bookmark, e-mail, etc. You can enter your weight for approximate calories burned, and turn on/off the elevation graph and mile markers.

title: Join Rideshare
content:
# Join Rideshare

Joining Rideshare is quick and simple.  Registration forms are available from the Rideshare office.  Once the information has been verified and approved by the Rideshare office, a brief orientation is required to explain the benefits you are entitled to as a member of the program.  The office must have an original copy of the registration form.

#### Eligibility Requirements

Members must meet the following requirements to enroll in the program.

* State, ASI or Foundation employee, part time or more
* 12 month permanent or probationary status
* Temporary lecturers with one quarter appointment or more
* Temporary appointment with 90 day contract or more
* Temporary emergency with 90 day contract or more
* You must share the ride at least 51% or more of the total trip distance
* Arrive to work on campus between 6 a.m. to 10 a.m., Monday through Friday

Students are not eligible to join the program.

**[[Click Here to Join Employee Rideshare]](https://app.smartsheet.com/b/form/369eda89d56242378935ec57fa2cfeef)**

title: Faculty/Staff Rideshare Benefits
content:
# Faculty/Staff Rideshare Benefits

Earn Bronco Bucks!
**Rideshare members earn Bronco Bucks for participating in the program.**

$1 a day:  Two person carpools, vanpool members
$2 per day:  Three or more member carpool, walkers, bicyclists, and vanpool drivers
$10 a month bonus:  Vanpool members who rideshare 80% or more days in a month

Members must complete the Daily Record each month to track their rideshare activity and will earn bronco bucks for only one commute options.  All incentives are considered taxable income and will be reported to the State Controller’s office each month.

**Check your Bronco Bucks Balance On-Line**You may check your account activity on your Bronco card at anytime!  Here’s how you do it.  Go to the GET page from the Foundation web site [here](https://get.cbord.com/csupomona/full/login.php "GET").  Enter your email address and password.  That’s it.  Log in anytime you want to check your balance or to see your spending activity.

**Guaranteed Ride Home for Rideshare Members**What if you rideshare and then have to leave for an unexpected emergency, illness, or have to work overtime?  Not to worry, we guarantee all our members a ride home in a taxi in the event of an emergency on the days they rideshare.  Our Guaranteed Ride Home Program ensures that you will not be stranded at work.

**Preferred Rideshare Parking for Members**Faculty/Staff rideshare parking spaces are designated on campus for carpools and vanpools.  A Rideshare parking sticker must be affixed to a regular parking permit to park in a space designated “Rideshare Parking.”

Semester Rideshare stickers are issued to members who are in possession of a semester or annual parking permit upon registration and when parking permits are issued. Rideshare stickers expire on the date indicated on the sticker. The Rideshare Office will only issue one (1) semester rideshare sticker per member. Stickers are not transferable.

Preferred Rideshare parking is available on a first-come, first-serve basis.  Rideshare spaces are located in Lot A, Lot F8, Lot J, and Lot S and are enforced during the hours of 7 a.m. to 1 p.m. Monday through Friday.  You may also park in the Clean Air Vehicle spaces in Parking Structure 2.  Any faculty/staff employee with a valid Cal Poly parking permit may park in faculty/staff rideshare spaces after 1 p.m.

Rideshare spaces are only to be used on the days that you carpool and are strictly enforced.  Please park in a regular faculty/staff parking space when you drive alone.  You must be an active rideshare member to receive this incentive.

**Transit Reimbursements**Metrolink and Amtrak riders are reimbursed on a semester basis to a maximum of $100.00 per month.  Bus riders are reimbursed on a semester basis to a maximum of $80.00 per month.  Reimbursement checks mailed to members home address.  Bus and train riders do not receive Bronco Bucks or Parking Reimbursements.

**Parking Reimbursements**Rideshare members who carpool, vanpool, bicycle, or walk and have purchased a parking permit qualify for a parking reimbursement.   Members will earn $.55 each commute day capped at $36/semester.  Members who rideshare at least 80% or more of the number of possible rideshare days in a semester will receive the maximum reimbursement of $36/semester.  Reimbursement checks will be mailed once a semester.

**Personalized Trip Planning**
Are you thinking of taking a trip on public transportation and not sure if you have the right information? We are here to help. We have the most current information and tips on commuting.

title: Rideshare Matchlist
content:
# Rideshare Matchlist

Looking for a carpool partner?  Contact the Rideshare Office.  We can send you a list of Cal Poly Pomona Faculty/Staff members who live in your area who you can contact to rideshare.

Faculty/Staff may contact the rideshare office directly or may send an email to rideshare@cpp.edu with the following information:
**In the subject line of the email please write, "Faculty/Staff Matchlist Requested"**

Name
Email Address (preferably a Cal Poly Pomona email address)
Home City
Home Zip Code

We may already have some rideshare members in our database who are already participating in our program.  If this is the case, we will forward a list to you via email.  The Rideshare office will also include some tips to get your carpool rolling!

For more information, please contact the Rideshare office.

title: Riding the Bus
content:
# Riding the Bus

Riding the bus to the campus is simple.  Foothill Transit buses make convenient stops at the corner of Temple Avenue and South Campus Drive.  From there, you can walk or ride the Bronco Express to the main part of the campus.

You can use the Foothill Transit trip planner software to assist you on-line with schedules and fares.  All you have to do is enter the location you are leaving from and the final destination, then enter the departure and arrival time for your trip.  Once that information is entered, a trip itinerary will appear.

[View the Foothill Transit Trip Planner](http://foothilltransit.org/trip-tools/trip-planner/ "Foothill Transit Trip Planner")

title: Metrolink Tickets for Students
content:
# Metrolink Tickets for Students

One advantage of being a student is the wide variety of discounts you may receive – including purchasing a Metrolink ticket at a discounted price.  Students receive a 25% discount off the regular adult fare for a one-way ticket, round-trip ticket, 7-day pass, or a monthly pass.  Student discount tickets or passes must be purchased at the Metrolink stations.  For help planning your trip stop by the Rideshare Office, we have maps and schedules and will share our expertise in commuting by rail.

title: Train and Bus Passes for Students
content: # Train and Bus Passes for Students
section: Train and Bus Passes for Students

title: Train and Bus Passes for Students
content:
## Bus Pass Information

The Rideshare office can assist students in finding the best route and schedules for taking Metro or Foothill Transit to Cal Poly Pomona.  Full-time students receive a discount when purchasing their pass.  Students must first obtain a date stamped from the Registrar's Office (located in the Student Services Building 121) and present it in person to a Metro or Foothill Transit location where they will be issued a transit photo ID.

Expand/Collapse All for faq number 1

title: Train and Bus Passes for Students
content:
## Foothill Transit and Metro College/Vocational TAP Card Information

Because we want you to get the most out of your college experience, we’ve partnered with Foothill Transit to bring you the Class Pass. The Class Pass is a pilot program that gives students at Cal Poly Pomona free and unlimited rides on Foothill Transit local and Silver Streak lines all semester long! Visit [foothilltransit.org/cpp](https://foothilltransit.org/cpp) to learn more.

Students are also able to get discounted Metro bus passes. Reduced fare passes are only available on a TAP card.  A TAP is a durable plastic card you can use again and again. If you’re a senior, disabled, or student rider, you can load your TAP card online, at a Foothill Transit Store, Metro Customer Center, pass vendors throughout the county, at any Metro Rail station, or through the new TAP app. Visit taptogo.net or call 1-866 TAP TOGO for more information.

The Foothill Transit Class Pass and LA Metro monthly passes can be reloaded at the **BRIC (Bronco Recreational Intramural Complex), Bldg 42.**

In addition, TAP offers you these benefits:

* As a reduced-fare rider, you are automatically signed up for free Balance Protection so you won't need to worry if  your card is lost or stolen.
* You can sign up for Autoload to automatically purchase your pass each month.
* TAP is a better choice for the environment because you reuse the same card each time.

Note: Existing Senior, Disabled and Student riders must obtain a TAP card to continue purchasing any kind of pass at the reduced rate.
section: Foothill Transit and Metro College/Vocational TAP Card Information

title: Train and Bus Passes for Students
content: ## How Do You Qualify?

title: Train and Bus Passes for Students
content:
### Foothill Transit Class Pass

All currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students with at least 1 credit unit at Cal Poly Pomona are eligible to get a Class Pass. Just bring your Bronco student ID to the **BRIC (Bronco Recreational Intramural Complex), Bldg 42,** and we’ll take care of the rest.

Continuing Education Unit (CEU) Online Learning and Certificate enrolled students are not eligible at this time.

title: Train and Bus Passes for Students
content:
### Metro Bus Riders

To qualify for a College/Vocational discount, you must complete the College/Vocational TAP ID application. The Rideshare Office, located in Building 35 - office 2131, has applications available for students.  Instructions are provided on the application.  Submit the completed application at any Metro Customer Center or mail it to:

Metro Reduced Fare Office

One Gateway Plaza, MS 99-PL-4

Los Angeles, CA, 90012-2952

Upon receipt of your College/Vocational TAP ID, you can load a pass onto your TAP card every month at any Metro Customer Center or area pass vendors.

Undergraduate and graduate students must provide proof of enrollment in an accredited school in Los Angeles County for a minimum of 12 units or 12 hours of in-classroom study per week for a minimum of 3 consecutive months. If you are enrolled in a summer program that meets for less than 3 months and/or 1 units, you can still qualify only if you met the eligibility requirements in the preceding spring term.

Please note that there is a non-refundable $1 application fee.  If applying by mail, please send check or money order made payable to Metro.

title: Train and Bus Passes for Students
content:
## Train Pass Information for Students

Metrolink fares are calculated with a distance-based formula using the shortest distance between stations.  To find out exactly what the fare will be for your trip, contact the Rideshare office or check out any of the following links:Automated ticket vending machine (located at every Metrolink station) Call 800-371-LINK (5465) <http://www.metrolinktrains.com/>

Students receive a 25% discount and may purchase tickets at the ticket vending machine at the Metrolink stations.

For schedules and information, visit Metrolink at <http://www.metrolinktrains.com/>

title: Vanpool
content:
# Vanpool

Let someone else do the driving.  Cal PolyPomona vanpools hold 7-10 people who share the commute to the campus.  Vanpools set their own route, operating schedule, and pick-up/ drop-off location.  Fares are determined by the number of passengers riding in the van, the monthly mileage, and fuel charges.  Drivers may also qualify for a free fare. All vanpool riders must complete a Passenger Agreement or Driver Agreement from the Rideshare Office before they ride the van.

[Click here](../docs/routes-for-web-2019-2020.pdf) for the current Cal Poly Pomona vanpools in service.

Full-time Foundation and ASI employees are eligible to ride vanpools.  **Students are not eligible for the vanpool program.**  Contact the Rideshare office for available seats on vanpools.

#### Vanpool Documents

* [Vanpool Passenger Agreement](https://www.cpp.edu/~parking/rideshare/faculty-staff-program/PASSENGER%20agreement%20Feb.%202014.pdf) (pdf)
* [Vanpool Driver Agreement](https://www.cpp.edu/~parking/rideshare/faculty-staff-program/DRIVER%20agreement%20Feb%20%202014.pdf)  (pdf)

title: Caltrans
content:
# Caltrans

The I-10 Freeway High Occupancy Vehicle Lane Project will add one carpool lane in both directions of the 10 Freeway from Citrus Avenue to the 57 Freeway. Work on the 5.2-mile section is scheduled to be completed in 2022.

Please plan accordingly to ensure safe and timely travel to campus.

Additional information about the project is available on the Caltrans District 7 website at [http://www.dot.ca.gov/d7/projects/10/10-hov.html](https://na01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.dot.ca.gov%2Fd7%2Fprojects%2F10%2F10-hov.html&data=02%7C01%7Cestherchou%40cpp.edu%7Cc9aa04cda86d49bd09b108d693763278%7C164ba61e39ec4f5d89ffaa1f00a521b4%7C0%7C0%7C636858533496455396&sdata=NuXVjl0dF2WpMI66lHfbh1gy0Uy2vVXyFHgTsTrMygI%3D&reserved=0).

Additional FAQs and a map of the closures is available on the university’s [Compas](https://www.cpp.edu/~compass/)[s](https://www.cpp.edu/~compass/) [website](https://www.cpp.edu/~compass/). Updates and more information, including alternate routes, can be found at the university’s [Compass website](https://www.cpp.edu/~compass/).

title: New Metrolink Connect Shuttle Service from Pomona North station available January 21, 2020!
content:
# New Metrolink Connect Shuttle Service from Pomona North station available January 21, 2020!

A new hourly round-trip shuttle service will be available on January 21, 2020 for San Bernardino Line riders arriving at the Pomona North station allowing riders to have more options to commute to Cal Poly Pomona.

As part of this schedule change, Cal Poly Pomona has also changed the stop and pick-up location to a more centrally location to connect to on-campus shuttles.  The stop will be located the Student Services Building at the traffic circle where Bronco Express shuttles route D and E stop.

A limited number of complimentary round-trip tickets will be available for students, faculty and staff to ride the Metrolink.  Please contact the Transportation Rideshare office at rideshare@cpp.edu or by phone at 909-869-4781.  Hurry, because these tickets will expire March 1, 2020.

*See the new schedule timetable effective January 21, 2020* *[here](2020metrolinkshuttle.pdf)*
